Japanese: ３月２２日　午前　９時４７分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: March 22, 9:47 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒が‥‥？
English: Adrian did it...?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。
English: ...That's what it looks like.

Japanese: まさか‥‥！　彼女にそんなこと、 できるわけがない。
English: Dude, no way! That woman couldn't do anything like that.

Japanese: 今日の法廷では、あなたに不利な 証拠がザクザク提出されますね。
English: In court today, there will be a mountain of evidence that will implicate you.

Japanese: ザクザク‥‥
English: A mountain of evidence...?

Japanese: だれかがあなたに、ツミを着せよう としているのは、まちがいない。
English: I'm certain there is someone out there trying very hard to pin this whole thing on you.

Japanese: おねがいしますよ、弁護士さん。 昨日も言いましたけど‥‥
English: Please, Mr. Lawyer! Dude, like I said yesterday...

Japanese: “春風のようにサワヤカなアイツ” ‥‥ですからね、ボク。
English: I'm "refreshing like a spring breeze", alright?

Japanese: スキャンダルだけは、ゼッタイに さけなくちゃいけないんですよ。
English: I can't let any sort of scandal ruin that.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: I understand...

Japanese: さあ‥‥そろそろ時間ね。
English: Well, it's almost time.

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥
English: Mia...

Japanese: 今日中にかならず、 無罪判決を勝ちとりましょう。
English: We must get a complete acquittal today.

Japanese: わかっています。
English: I know...

Japanese: これ以上‥‥真宵ちゃんに、 ツラい思いをさせられません。
English: I can't focus on Maya's situation right now.

Japanese: ‥‥それに、春美ちゃんにも。
English: ...Or Pearls' either.

Japanese: 何がなんでも、今日中に ネジふせて見せます！
English: No matter what, I have to focus on winning this case by the end of the day!

Japanese: ‥‥そうね。 それで行きましょう！
English: Indeed. Well, let's get going!

Japanese: ‥‥ピピピピピピ‥‥
English: ...*beep beep beep*...
(It's him!)

Japanese: ‥‥こちら、成歩堂！
English: This is Wright!

Japanese: おはようございます。 今日はいよいよ、本番ですね。
English: Good morning. This is it, Mr. Attorney; the day of the trial.

Japanese: ま、真宵ちゃんは‥‥ 真宵ちゃんはブジだろうな！
English: Maya... She's unharmed, right!?

Japanese: さあ‥‥
English: Well...

Japanese: 今朝のぞいてみたのですが、 ぐったりしてましたよ。
English: When I checked on her earlier this morning, she seemed a bit, how shall we say, tired.

Japanese: だいじょうぶ。人は そうカンタンには死にません。
English: Don't worry. People don't die that easily.

Japanese: それより、今日の勝負。 それだけを考えることです。
English: Besides, what you really should be concentrating on, is winning today's trial.

Japanese: く‥‥くそッ‥‥
English: G...Grrrr...

Japanese: 私としても、あなたにはぜひ、 勝ってもらわなければなりません。
English: For myself, you must win today's trial.

Japanese: そこで‥‥
English: Which is why...

Japanese: 今朝、あなたに１つ、 プレゼントを用意しました。
English: I sent you a little present this morning.

Japanese: プレゼント？ なんのことだ、いったい‥‥
English: Present? What in the world would you want to give me!?

Japanese: 法廷が始まれば、 わかるでしょう。イヤでも。
English: You'll figure it out once the trial opens... even if you don't "like" my gift...

Japanese: こころよく受けとって ‥‥そして、勝ってください。
English: I expect you to graciously accept it, and win the day's contest... If you please.

Japanese: ちょっと待て！
English: Wait!

Japanese: （ユーカイ犯がぼくに “プレゼント”だと‥‥？）
English: (The kidnapper sent me a "present"...?)

Japanese: 弁護士さん。 ‥‥今のは‥‥？
English: Mr. Lawyer dude? Who was that...?

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥ベツに。あなたには ゼンゼンカンケイありませんから。
English: Ah, um... No one. It has nothing to do with you, so forget you heard anything!

Japanese: ‥‥ウソっぽいなあ。
English: ...Dude, did your nose just get longer?

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第３法廷
English: March 22, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 3

Japanese: これより、王都楼　真悟の 法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Matt Engarde.

Japanese: 検察側・弁護側とも、 準備のほうはよろしいですか？
English: Are the prosecution and defense ready?

Japanese: 弁護側、準備完了しております。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: コラッ！　弁護人！ 狩魔検事はどうしたんですか！
English: I say! Mr. Wright! What happened to Ms. von Karma!?

Japanese: しし、知りませんよ！ （なんでぼくに怒るんだよ！）
English: I-I don't know, Your Honor! (Why are you getting mad at me!?)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長どのッ！
English: ...Your Honor!

Japanese: 静かになさい、係官。 開廷中ですぞ。
English: Please be quiet, bailiff. Court is in session.

Japanese: 用件は手みじかにおねがいします。 ‥‥なんですかな？
English: If you must tell me something, please keep it brief. Now then, what is it?

Japanese: ‥‥検事が‥‥狩魔検事が‥‥
English: Prosecutor... Prosecutor von Karma has...

Japanese: 今朝、ナニモノかに‥‥ 狙撃されました！
English: This morning, Ms. von Karma was shot by an unknown gunman!

Japanese: ななななんですってェェェッ！
English: WWWWWWWHAT!?

Japanese: そ‥‥狙撃だって！
English: Sh-Shot!?

Japanese: ‥‥どうやらこれが‥‥
English: Somehow...

Japanese: あの男が言ってた “プレゼント”というわけね。
English: I think this is the "present" that man was talking about.

Japanese: “プレゼント”‥‥！
English: His "present"...!

Japanese: 狩魔検事は、今、この国で もっとも優秀な検事よ。
English: Ms. von Karma is one of the top prosecutors in the country at the moment.

Japanese: その検事を‥‥消した。
English: If she disappeared...

Japanese: これであなたは‥‥ “有利になった”ということ。
English: This would be to your "advantage".

Japanese: （そ‥‥そんな‥‥ まさかそんな、バカな！）
English: (Th-This... This is totally insane!)

Japanese: か‥‥狩魔検事は！ 彼女のイノチは‥‥！
English: M-Ms. von Karma! Is she alright!?

Japanese: わ、私に聞かれても‥‥
English: I don't have that answer!

Japanese: ‥‥イノチに別状はない。 狩魔　冥は生きている。
English: She's alive and in stable condition.

Japanese: そうですか‥‥ ホッとしました。
English: That's good... Phew.
Y-Y-You're...

Japanese: （やはり‥‥来たか‥‥）
English: (I thought he'd show up...)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長。
English: ...Your Honor...

Japanese: 狩魔　冥はツゴウにより、 出廷できない。
English: Due to the circumstances, Ms. Franziska von Karma can not appear in court today.

Japanese: 代理で出廷した ‥‥検事・御剣　怜侍。
English: I, Miles Edgeworth, will be taking her place.

Japanese: 検察側の準備は‥‥ 無論、完了しているッ！
English: The prosecution is ready... naturally.

Japanese: 狩魔検事は右の肩に銃弾を受け、 現在、手術中だ。
English: Ms. von Karma was shot in her right shoulder, and is currently undergoing surgery.

Japanese: さいわい私は、この事件の すべてのデータに目を通している。
English: Luckily, I have looked this case over and am familiar with the details.

Japanese: ‥‥私なりのやりかたで、 王都楼　真悟の罪を立証しよう。
English: The prosecution seeks to prove the guilt of Mr. Matt Engarde.

Japanese: わ、わかりました‥‥
English: Th-The court acknowledges the prosecution.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。
English: ...Wright.

Japanese: この１年の旅で、 私がたどりついたコタエ‥‥
English: I finally found the answer I was struggling for on my long journey this past year.

Japanese: この事件が終了したとき、 キミもそれを知るだろう。
English: By the time this case comes to an end, you too, will know the answer.

Japanese: それでは最初の証人‥‥、
English: Now then, the prosecution would like to call its first witness...

Japanese: イトノコギリ刑事を 入廷させていただこう！
English: Please bring Detective Gumshoe to the witness stand!

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 名前と職業を。
English: Witness, your name and occupation.

Japanese: 名前は糸鋸　圭介ッス。
English: My name's Dick Gumshoe, sir.

Japanese: とりあえず今は、 所轄署の刑事‥‥ッス。
English: I'm a detective down at the precinct... for now.

Japanese: “とりあえず”‥‥？
English: "For now"...?

Japanese: この法廷が終了したら‥‥ 辞表を提出するッス。
English: After this trial's over... I'm supposed to turn in my badge, sir.

Japanese: （い、イトノコ刑事‥‥）
English: (D-Detective Gumshoe...)

Japanese: ‥‥ショボくれた証人は 必要ない。
English: The prosecution has no need for a depressed witness.

Japanese: カオを上げたまえ。 イトノコギリ刑事！
English: Lift your head up and face forward like a proud officer, Detective Dick Gumshoe!

Japanese: は‥‥はッ！
English: Y-Yes, sir!

Japanese: さっそく証言をしてもらおう。
English: Now, let's have your testimony.

Japanese: この事件のポイントを押さえたい。 まずはそこから、おねがいする。
English: If we want to explore the various facets of this case, we must start with that.

Japanese: ‥‥カクゴなさい、なるほどくん。 キビシイ戦いになるわ。
English: Get ready, Phoenix. This is going to be one very rough fight.

Japanese: ええ。‥‥なにしろ、 相手がアイツですからね。
English: Yeah... It would have to be with Edgeworth as my opponent.

Japanese: （“たどりついたコタエ” ‥‥おもしろい）
English: (The answer he was "struggling" for... Interesting...)

Japanese: （見せてもらおうか‥‥）
English: (Show me this "answer" you finally found, Edgeworth...)

Japanese: ～事件のポイント～
English: -- Bare Facts of the Case --

Japanese: 事件は、ヒーロー・オブ・ヒーロー 授賞式のあと、発生したッス。
English: This murder happened after the Hero of Heroes award ceremony, sir.

Japanese: 被害者の藤見野　イサオは 控え室で、死体で見つかったッス。
English: The victim, Juan Corrida, was found dead in his hotel room.

Japanese: 死因の特定に時間がかかったッスが 他殺なのはまちがいないッス。
English: After looking into the cause of death, we believe he was definitely murdered, sir.

Japanese: 当初、カラのギターケースが 不審な点と考えられていたッス。
English: At first, we thought there was something suspicious about the empty guitar case.

Japanese: その後、ギターケースは、事件とは 関係ないことがわかったッス。
English: However, we later found out that the guitar case had nothing to do with the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 授賞式が終わったあと、 被害者はひとりで控え室に‥‥？
English: After the award ceremony ended, the victim was alone in his room...?

Japanese: はッ！
English: Yes, sir!

Japanese: 被告人・被害者とも、それぞれ 自分の控え室にひとりだったッス。
English: Both the victim and defendant went alone to their rooms, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですか。 では、弁護人。おねがいします。
English: I see. Mr. Wright, you may begin your cross-examination.

Japanese: ～事件のポイント～
English: -- Bare Facts of the Case --

Japanese: 事件は、ヒーロー・オブ・ヒーロー 授賞式のあと、発生したッス。
English: This murder happened after the Hero of Heroes award ceremony, sir.

Japanese: 被害者の藤見野　イサオは 控え室で、死体で見つかったッス。
English: The victim, Juan Corrida, was found dead in his hotel room.

Japanese: 死因の特定に時間がかかったッスが 他殺なのはまちがいないッス。
English: After looking into the cause of death, we believe he was definitely murdered, sir.

Japanese: 当初、カラのギターケースが 不審な点と考えられていたッス。
English: At first, we thought there was something suspicious about the empty guitar case.

Japanese: その後、ギターケースは、事件とは 関係ないことがわかったッス。
English: However, we later found out that the guitar case had nothing to do with the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥まずは、情報を集めましょう。
English: Our first order of business should be to gather more info from his testimony.

Japanese: どこかに、ワナが‥‥？
English: But there might be a trap set up for us somewhere...

Japanese: あるいは、ね。
English: Maybe.

Japanese: でも、ワナにはまるのも、 弁護士のシゴトよ。
English: However, setting off traps is a part of a lawyer's job too.

Japanese: はい‥‥
English: If you say so...

Japanese: そしてもちろん、 そこから抜け出すのも、ね。
English: Of course, we're supposed to escape from them too, wouldn't you say?

Japanese: 授賞式の前後、 その時間の関係は‥‥？
English: Would you please give us a brief timeline of what happened after the ceremony?

Japanese: そッスね。授賞式が始まったのが 午後６時ッス。
English: OK, pal. The ceremony started at 6 PM.

Japanese: 終了したのが８時ごろで‥‥ そこで休憩に入ったッス。
English: It ended around 8 PM... and then there was a short break.

Japanese: ３０分後、ロビーで受賞記念の ショーが始まるはずだったッス。
English: A special post-ceremony show was supposed to start in the lobby 30 minutes later.

Japanese: そこで、死体が発見された ‥‥というわけですか。
English: And that's when the victim's body was found, correct?

Japanese: 事件は、その休憩時間のあいだに 起こった、ということだ。
English: Which is to say, the murder occurred during that thirty minute break period.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ つづけてください、証人。
English: Hmm... Please continue with your testimony, Detective.

Japanese: 死体を発見したのは‥‥ 霧緒さんでしたね？
English: The person who discovered the victim's body was Adrian Andrews, correct?

Japanese: ‥‥そッス。
English: ...Yeah.

Japanese: どなたですか？ キリオさん、というのは。
English: Who is this "Adrian Andrews" you're talking about?

Japanese: 被告人・王都楼　真悟の マネージャーで、美人ッス。
English: She's the defendant, Matt Engarde's, manager. She's a really pretty lady... sir.

Japanese: びじん‥‥はあ、 女のかたですか‥‥。
English: Ah, so she's a pretty lady... I wonder if she will grace us with her presence...

Japanese: 彼女、ショーが始まる時間になった ので、オートロを呼びにきたッス。
English: When the post-ceremony show was about to start, she went to get Mr. Engarde.

Japanese: そのついでに、被害者の控え室も のぞいたッス。
English: After visiting his room, she next went to the victim's room to get him for the show, sir.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥そこで、死体を 発見したわけですか‥‥。
English: I see... And that's when she found the victim's body...

Japanese: 死因‥‥って、ムネに刺さっていた ナイフじゃないんですか？
English: The cause of death... Wasn't that because Mr. Corrida was stabbed in the chest...?

Japanese: そう考えるのが、シロートの アサハカさ、ってやつッス。
English: Only a careless amateur would believe something so brainless as that, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥現場写真を よく見てほしいッス。
English: Take a good, hard look at the crime photo.

Japanese: プロが注目するのは、 このマフラーッス。
English: Now, a real pro's attention would be drawn here, to this bandana.

Japanese: “マフラーッス”‥‥ですか？
English: Mmm... "Banana"...

Japanese: ‥‥クビにぐるぐる巻きに なってる布きれのコトッス。
English: Um, his "bandana", sir... That's the thing wrapped tightly around his neck, sir.

Japanese: ああ、はいはい。エリマキね。
English: Ah, yes, yes. I see. His banana-scented bandana.

Japanese: じゃあ、ナイフは‥‥？
English: Then, what about the knife...?

Japanese: 被害者の死後、念を入れて 刺されたらしいッス。
English: It seems to have been stuck in the victim's chest on purpose after his death.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。 用心深いハンニンですね。
English: Hmm... We have a crafty murderer on our hands here.

Japanese: 証拠品《解剖記録》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Autopsy Report added to the Court Record.

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: And why did you think that?

Japanese: いや、だってカラッスから。
English: Because it was empty, pal.

Japanese: 忍者ナンジャは、まっかなギターが トレードマークだったッス。
English: The Jammin' Ninja doesn't go anywhere without his bright red guitar.

Japanese: それが、現場から 発見されなかったッス。
English: And we couldn't find it anywhere at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: あっ、こういうのはどうですか！
English: Oh! Then how about this theory!

Japanese: ファンのひとりが、ギターほしさに 犯行におよんだ。‥‥どうです！
English: A fan really wanted the guitar and did the crime to get it! How's that!?

Japanese: ‥‥ワレワレも それは考えたッス。しかし‥‥
English: Um, we thought of that too... But...

Japanese: しかし？
English: But...?

Japanese: ギターケースには、被害者の 指紋しか残されていなかったッス。
English: The only fingerprints on the guitar case were the victim's.

Japanese: 被害者の指紋だけ‥‥
English: Only the victim's, huh...?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そうですか。 ちょっと、ザンネンですな。
English: Hmm... I see. Ah, so much for my theory then...

Japanese: 証拠品《ギターケース》の データを書きなおした。
English: Guitar Case updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: 関係ないというコンキョは？
English: What convinced you it had nothing to do with the case?

Japanese: あの夜、現場のホテル・バンドーに ギターは存在しなかったッス。
English: The guitar wasn't at the Gatewater Hotel that night.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: Well then, where was it?

Japanese: まっかなギターは、ケッキョク テレビ局のスタジオにあったッス。
English: The bright red guitar was eventually found at the TV studio.

Japanese: 被害者の藤見野　イサオは、 ケースだけ持ってきてたッス。
English: The victim, Juan Corrida, had apparently only taken the case with him, sir.

Japanese: 中身を入れ忘れた、 ということですか‥‥？
English: So you mean he forgot to put the guitar inside the case...?

Japanese: そッス。授賞式のステージでも、 ギターは持っていなかったッス。
English: Yes, sir. Even when he was onstage for the ceremony, he didn't have his guitar.

Japanese: ギターケースは、最初から カラだったわけですか‥‥
English: So that guitar case was empty even before he got to the hotel...

Japanese: そのとおりッス。 事件にカンケイしようがないッス。
English: Yeah, that's right. So it really had nothing to do with the case after all.

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: ‥‥そんなところで じゅうぶんだろう。
English: I believe that is enough.

Japanese: 被害者はまず、マフラーで クビを絞められた。
English: First, the victim was choked to death with his bandana.

Japanese: そして絶命したのち、犯人は 念を入れて、ナイフを刺した。
English: Then, after the victim was dead, the killer deliberately stabbed him with a knife.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: それでは、次に進もう。
English: Which brings me to my next point.

Japanese: 警察がなぜ、 王都楼　真悟を逮捕したか‥‥？
English: Why then, did the police arrest Matt Engarde...?

Japanese: そのコンキョについて、だ。
English: Because there was reason enough to suspect him.

Japanese: （ついに、来たか‥‥）
English: (Here it comes... Looks like Edgeworth's back in full swing...)

Japanese: では、証人。 証言をおねがいします。
English: Very well. Detective Gumshoe, please testify about this matter.

Japanese: はッ！
English: Yes, sir!

Japanese: ～王都楼を逮捕した理由～
English: -- Why Arrest Engarde? --

Japanese: 王都楼と被害者の藤見野　イサオは ライバルの関係だったッス。
English: Matt Engarde and Juan Corrida were huge rivals with each other.

Japanese: たがいの存在がジャマだった‥‥ それが、動機ッス。
English: They each thought the other guy was "in his way". That's motive enough in my book.

Japanese: そして、証拠。‥‥まず、 忍者ナンジャのボタンがあるッス。
English: As for evidence... There's the Jammin' Ninja's button.

Japanese: 衣装からちぎれたボタンが、王都楼 のハカマから発見されたッス。
English: It was ripped off of the ninja costume and was found in Mr. Engarde's "hakama".

Japanese: さらに、凶器のナイフには、被告の 指紋がハッキリ残っていたッス。
English: The defendant's fingerprints were also all over the knife.

Japanese: 被告は、ナイフを買って準備して いた‥‥これは、計画殺人ッス！
English: The defendant bought the knife for the crime... Which makes this a premeditated murder!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥凶器のナイフに、 被告人の指紋が‥‥。
English: Hmm... So the defendant's fingerprints were found on the knife used in the stabbing.

Japanese: べとべとになるほど 指紋まみれだったッス。
English: It was sort of sticky on the handle, so the fingerprints came out pretty clearly, sir.

Japanese: 証拠品《ナイフ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Knife added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥そして、このボタンッス。
English: ...And there's this button.

Japanese: それが、被告人の衣装から 発見されたわけですね。
English: That was found in the defendant's clothes, was it?

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥これもまた 血でよごれていますが‥‥？
English: Hmm... And is this button also covered in blood...?

Japanese: その血痕は‥‥被害者のもので あることがわかったッス。
English: Yes, and we know that the blood on it is the victim's blood, sir.

Japanese: えええええッ！
English: What!?

Japanese: 証拠品《忍者ナンジャのボタン》の データを法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Jammin' Ninja's Button added to the Court Record.

Japanese: これは‥‥もう、 決定的ですね。
English: All of this points very clearly to the defendant, doesn't it?

Japanese: まさにそのとおり。 ‥‥いかがかな。弁護人？
English: Yes, it most certainly does, Your Honor. Ready to give in yet, Wright?

Japanese: ‥‥フ、フン！　穴だらけですね！
English: Hmph! I'll find the hole in your argument somehow!

Japanese: 強がりはケッコウ。 さっさとムダな尋問をしたまえ。
English: You can press as hard as you'd like. Just hurry up with your usual pointless questions.

Japanese: ‥‥うううううう‥‥
English: Grrrrrrrrr...

Japanese: ～王都楼を逮捕した理由～
English: -- Why Arrest Engarde? --

Japanese: 王都楼と被害者の藤見野　イサオは ライバルの関係だったッス。
English: Matt Engarde and Juan Corrida were huge rivals with each other.

Japanese: たがいの存在がジャマだった‥‥ それが、動機ッス。
English: They each thought the other guy was "in his way". That's motive enough in my book.

Japanese: そして、証拠。‥‥まず、 忍者ナンジャのボタンがあるッス。
English: As for evidence... There's the Jammin' Ninja's button.

Japanese: 衣装からちぎれたボタンが、王都楼 のハカマから発見されたッス。
English: It was ripped off of the ninja costume and was found in Mr. Engarde's "hakama".

Japanese: さらに、凶器のナイフには、被告の 指紋がハッキリ残っていたッス。
English: The defendant's fingerprints were also all over the knife.

Japanese: 被告は、ナイフを買って準備して いた‥‥これは、計画殺人ッス！
English: The defendant bought the knife for the crime... Which makes this a premeditated murder!

Japanese: （‥‥血のついた被害者のボタンに ナイフの指紋‥‥か！）
English: (A button covered in the victim's blood... And a knife with Engarde's fingerprints!)

Japanese: 気の早い裁判官なら、もう木槌を たたいているところね。
English: Be grateful. If the judge were more rash, he would've already pounded his gavel in closing.

Japanese: ‥‥まいったな‥‥。
English: We're still in a world of trouble...

Japanese: たたかれる前に、 指摘してやりましょう。
English: Well, before any battle, you must find your enemy's weakness.

Japanese: たとえ小さなムジュンでも、ね。
English: So let's find the weakness in this testimony, no matter how small it may be, OK Phoenix?

Japanese: 人気では、オートロさんが 勝っていたんですよね。
English: But in terms of popularity, Mr. Engarde won, did he not?

Japanese: ‥‥そッス。 フシギなコトに‥‥
English: ...Yeah. But you know what's ironic, pal?

Japanese: 藤見野　イサオは、いつも オートロに一歩、およばないッス。
English: Juan Corrida was always one step behind Mr. Engarde in everything.

Japanese: だから、今回の忍者ナンジャは まさに最後の大勝負だったッス。
English: This year it seemed like he'd finally caught up, ready for the big, final showdown.

Japanese: でも、結局‥‥イサオさんは グランプリを逃した‥‥。
English: But Mr. Corrida lost the Grand Prix in the end...

Japanese: それはそれは、 くやしかったでしょうねえ。
English: That is too bad... He must have been pretty downhearted after losing.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Wait just one second here!

Japanese: オートロさんの人気は、 イサオさんに勝っていたハズです！
English: Mr. Engarde was beating Mr. Corrida in the popularity polls!

Japanese: ま、そッスけどね‥‥。
English: Well, yeah, I guess, but...

Japanese: ‥‥つまり、被告人にとって 被害者は、ライバルではなかった！
English: Which means that in the defendant's eyes, the victim was not a rival at all!

Japanese: 殺害の動機にはなりません！
English: Which means he had no motive to kill at all!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ それは、そのとおりです。
English: Hmm... Yes, I quite agree.

Japanese: いかがですか？　証人。
English: Well, Detective?

Japanese: ハ。いや、それはそのお‥‥
English: Um, it's not... Well...

Japanese: でも、そう考えないと、被告に 動機がなくなっちゃうッスから‥‥
English: I guess if you put it that way, then yeah, the defendant would've had no motive...

Japanese: ‥‥刑事。キサマがクビになる 理由もわかる気がする。
English: Detective. I'm beginning to see why you were fired.

Japanese: え！　みみ、御剣検事まで そんな‥‥
English: Eh!? N-No! Not you too, Mr. Edgeworth, sir! That's...

Japanese: 退職金の査定‥‥ 楽しみにしておくことだ。
English: I look forward to your pension negotiations.

Japanese: ななな、なんスとおおおォォォッ！
English: N-N-N-NOOOOOOOOOOO!

Japanese: まあまあ、証人。 とりあえず、証言を。
English: Now, now, Detective. Let's continue with the testimony.

Japanese: まさか、退職金まで‥‥
English: *sniffle* Noooo... Not my poor pension too...

Japanese: 刑事！　カネがほしければ つづけたまえッ！
English: Detective! If you value your money, I suggest you proceed!

Japanese: は‥‥はッ！　とりあえず、 ドーキはそんなところッス！
English: Y-Yes, sir! We can talk about my pension later, sir!

Japanese: （‥‥今、モンダイ発言が なかったか‥‥？）
English: (Um, what about what I was saying...? Hello? Anyone...?)

Japanese: そのボタンですが‥‥ 被害者のものだという証拠は？
English: Do you have any proof that button belonged to the victim?

Japanese: え？　イミがわかんねッス。
English: Huh? What do you mean, pal?

Japanese: いやいやいや。
English: Oh. Umm, let me put it this way...

Japanese: “このボタンが、忍者ナンジャの 衣装からむしり取られた”
English: I'm asking you if you have any evidence to back up your claim that,

Japanese: ‥‥それを立証する証拠は あるかと聞いているんです。
English: "this button was ripped off of the Jammin' Ninja's costume".

Japanese: え。‥‥でも、見た感じ そっくりッスよ？
English: Huh? But can't you tell by just looking at it?

Japanese: そ、それに、被害者の血が ついてるし。
English: A-And the victim's blood is on it.

Japanese: 血ぐらい、だれかが コスりつけたのかもしれません。
English: Anyone could have smeared that blood on there afterward.

Japanese: みみ、ミツルギ検事‥‥ 助けてほしいッス！
English: M-M-Mr. Edgeworth... Help me... sir!

Japanese: （‥‥よし！　やっぱり、 そんな証拠なんか‥‥）
English: (Alright! I knew it had to be this piece of evidence. Now to reel this one in...)
...Thread.

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: ボタンは当然、糸で衣装に ぬいつけられている。
English: The button was attached to the costume by thread, obviously.

Japanese: それが、むしり取られたわけだ。
English: And that thread snapped when the button was torn off.

Japanese: 衣装がわの糸と、 ボタンがわの糸の先端‥‥
English: If you match up the ends of the thread on the costume with the thread on the button...

Japanese: ほつれている部分を照合した ところ、両者は完全に一致した。
English: it's a perfect match.

Japanese: そッス！　ピッタリ一致したッス！
English: Yeah, that's it! They're a perfect match, pal!

Japanese: う。
English: Urk.

Japanese: さすが、御剣検事ね ‥‥スキがないわ。
English: That's Edgeworth for you. Never misses a beat.

Japanese: そのボタン‥‥ いつ、見つかったんですか？
English: When was this button found?

Japanese: 死体が発見されて、ワレワレは すぐ、関係者を集めたッス。
English: Pretty soon after the body was found, we rounded up everyone who knew Mr. Corrida.

Japanese: そして、全員の身体検査を。 ボタンは、そのとき発見したッス。
English: And then we did a search on them all. That's when we found the button.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ほぼ、事件の 直後ですね‥‥
English: Hmm... So it was almost immediately after the murder...

Japanese: 小細工するヒマなど、 なかったはずだな‥‥。
English: The police didn't have the free time to lollygag and play tricks, unlike some people...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった‥‥）
English: (Hey! What is he trying to say about me here?)

Japanese: 指紋は、どういうふうに ついていましたか？
English: How were the fingerprints arranged on the knife?

Japanese: え？　イミがわかんねッス。
English: Huh? What do you mean, pal?

Japanese: 指紋を調べれば、被告がナイフを どう持っていたか、わかります。
English: By examining the fingerprints, you can determine how the defendant held the knife.

Japanese: ふつうに持っていたか、それとも 逆手に持っていたか‥‥
English: For example, did he hold it normally, or overhand...?

Japanese: あ、なるほど！ その発想はなかったッスねー。
English: Oh! Is that what you meant!? Well, we didn't actually think of that...

Japanese: （カンシンされてしまった‥‥）
English: (I can't believe the bumbling of this department...)

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。 証言を聞いていなかったのか？
English: ...Hopeless. Were you paying attention to the testimony, Wright?

Japanese: ナイフには、被告人の指紋が べたべたついていた。
English: The defendant's fingerprints were "all over" the knife.

Japanese: 犯行の瞬間、どう持っていたか 推測ができないほどに、な。
English: There is no way to determine how the knife was held at the moment of the murder.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そのナイフ、被告の ものだった、ということですか？
English: Hmm... So is the defendant the owner of this knife, then?

Japanese: ナイフを買っていたからといって、 計画殺人とはかぎりません！
English: There is no way this was a premeditated murder, even if he bought the knife!

Japanese: ザンネンながら、このナイフは 折りたためるタイプではないッス。
English: Sorry, pal. This isn't just some pocket knife.

Japanese: こんなモノ、用もなく 持ち歩くヤツ、いないッス。
English: It's not really useful for anything, and you can't just walk around with it either.

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (Argh. Well, this is not good...)

Japanese: （“計画殺人”と立証されたら おしまいだぞ‥‥）
English: (If the prosecution can prove it was a premeditated murder, we're done for...)

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: ...Phoenix.

Japanese: は、はい‥‥？
English: Y-Yes?

Japanese: 今の証言‥‥あからさまに 気になるところがあるわ。
English: There's something very interesting about what the detective said just now.

Japanese: よく考えてみましょう。 ‥‥手おくれにならないうちに。
English: Think carefully, before it's too late.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Wait a second!

Japanese: な‥‥なんスか‥‥？
English: Wh-What?

Japanese: 《計画殺人》‥‥そのコンキョは、
English: So the basis of your argument that this was a premeditated murder is simply that

Japanese: “あらかじめナイフを買っていた” からですか。
English: my client "bought a knife beforehand"?

Japanese: そのとおりッス。 このナイフは、被害者が‥‥
English: That's right, pal. The defendant...

Japanese: 買ったものではないんですよ。
English: ...Did not buy this knife.

Japanese: ど、どういうコトッス‥‥？
English: H-Huh!?

Japanese: ナイフの柄をよく見れば、 すぐわかることです。
English: Take a good look at the handle of this knife, and you'll know what I'm talking about.
...Huh?

Japanese: “バンドー”‥‥と 刻印されているようですね‥‥
English: It has a "Gatewater" seal set into the handle...

Japanese: ‥‥バンドー‥‥？ どこかで聞いたような。
English: "Gatewater"...? I think I've heard that name somewhere before.

Japanese: ホテルの名前ですよ。 “ホテル・バンドー”。
English: That's the name of the hotel. The Gatewater Hotel.
Uh oh.

Japanese: 凶器のナイフは ホテルのものだった！
English: The murder knife was actually property of the hotel!

Japanese: 犯行は、計画的なものでは なかったのです！
English: Which means this murder was not premeditated!

Japanese: たしかに、そうなります！ これは、大きな‥‥
English: Yes, that is very true! This is a very big...

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥
English: Heh heh heh...

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんですか？ 御剣検事。
English: Wh-What is it, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥シツレイ。弁護人が あまりに必死だったもので。
English: I'm sorry, but the defense is simply too careless.

Japanese: なんだと‥‥！
English: What!?

Japanese: 犯行が《計画的》だったかどうか？ ‥‥わかりきったことだ。
English: I think whether the crime was premeditated or not... has already been determined.

Japanese: ど、どういうことだ‥‥！
English: H-How so!?

Japanese: ‥‥たしかにこのナイフは、 あのホテルのものだ。
English: I admit this knife is hotel property.

Japanese: そんなことに気づかない バカは、警察にはいない。
English: There is no one currently on the police force that is dumb enough not to realize this.

Japanese: ‥‥気づかなかったッス。
English: ...But I didn't kn... Oh... *whimper*

Japanese: 問題は。
English: The question is...

Japanese: そのナイフが、いったい どこにあったものなのか‥‥？
English: Where did this knife come from?

Japanese: そ、それはモチロン、被害者の イサオさんの部屋、なのでは‥‥
English: Wh-Why that's obvious! It came from the victim, Mr. Corrida's room...

Japanese: ザンネンながら、そうではない。
English: Sorry, Your Honor, but that is incorrect.

Japanese: ‥‥被害者は、殺害される前に 食事をとっている。
English: The victim ate a last meal before he was murdered. With that being the case...

Japanese: しかし‥‥テーブルの上に 注目していただきたい。
English: I would like to draw the court's attention to what is on top of the table.

Japanese: ナイフとフォークが‥‥ そろっていますッ！
English: There is a knife and a fork on the table!

Japanese: ‥‥では、いったい！
English: Then...

Japanese: このナイフは、どこから‥‥！
English: Where in the world did this knife come from!?

Japanese: ここで‥‥
English: If it pleases the court...

Japanese: 今度は被告人・王都楼　真悟の 控え室を思い出してほしい。
English: I would like for us to recall the room of the defendant, Mr. Matt Engarde.

Japanese: ‥‥テーブルの上、だ。
English: Especially what was on top of his table...

Japanese: ‥‥足りないものがある。 ナイフが、１本。
English: There is something missing... Perhaps, it is a single knife?

Japanese: 我々は、残された食器の 指紋を調べた。
English: We investigated the leftover dishes for fingerprints, and while we were investigating,

Japanese: ‥‥足りないのは、 王都楼　真悟のナイフだった。
English: we came to the conclusion that Mr. Matt Engarde's knife was missing.
Urk...

Japanese: 王都楼は、食事に使ったナイフを 持って、被害者の控え室に行った。
English: Mr. Engarde had gone to the victim's room with the knife he had used during dinner.

Japanese: ではなぜ、ナイフを持っていたか？ ‥‥殺意があったから、だ。
English: Why would he carry a knife on a visit? To kill, of course.

Japanese: ‥‥以上。この犯行が計画的 だったことを‥‥
English: And with that, I believe the prosecution has proven...

Japanese: 私なりに、立証してみた。
English: This was a premeditated murder.

Japanese: さすが、御剣検事です。 アザヤカな立証でした。
English: Amazing, Mr. Edgeworth. Absolutely brilliant. A brilliantly clear deduction.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥最初から 仕組まれていたみたいね。
English: It seems like Edgeworth had this planned from the very beginning...
(This must be one of those "traps", and I just walked headlong into it!)

Japanese: 凶器に残っていた指紋。 被害者の衣装からちぎれたボタン。
English: A murder weapon with fingerprints, and a button from the victim's costume.

Japanese: ‥‥証拠はじゅうぶんですね。
English: There is quite a sizable amount of evidence here.

Japanese: これ以上の審理は、 ムダだと断言できる。
English: I can safely say that any further deliberation is a waste of Your Honor's time.

Japanese: もっとも‥‥
English: Although...

Japanese: 弁護側に、まだ提出すべき 証拠でもあれば、ベツだが、な。
English: I wouldn't mind if the defense were to present evidence not yet shown to the court.

Japanese: （提出すべき‥‥証拠？）
English: (Evidence not yet shown...?)

Japanese: 当然‥‥まだ審理されていない 重要な証拠品、ということね。
English: He means evidence that the court hasn't seen yet. In other words, new evidence.

Japanese: 弁護人。いかがですか。
English: What does the defense have to say about this, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: そ、そうですね‥‥
English: Um, well...

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: 裁判長の心証は、かぎりなく 検察側にかたむいているわ。
English: The judge is favoring the prosecution right now.

Japanese: ここでいいかげんな返事を したら‥‥
English: If we answer with something wrong here...

Japanese: （その瞬間 ‥‥木槌はふりおろされる！）
English: (That gavel of his will be ringing out to the sound of our defeat!)

Japanese: 弁護人。審理を必要とする重要な 証拠品、何か提出できますかな？
English: Mr. Wright, do you have some- thing important and necessary to present to this court?

Japanese: （これはきっと、ワナに ちがいない‥‥）
English: (This has to be another trap!)

Japanese: （ヘタな証拠を出すより、 ここはダマっていたほうが‥‥）
English: (Better if I don't say any- thing than risk throwing out a bad piece of evidence.)

Japanese: どうやら弁護側は、 グウの音も出ないようですね。
English: Looks like the defense isn't saying a peep on this one.

Japanese: ‥‥つまり。審理は これでじゅうぶんということだ。
English: Which means this court is adjourned.

Japanese: なるほどくん！ ‥‥同じ“負け”なら、
English: Phoenix! We will lose this case if you give up here!

Japanese: せめて、あなたなりの グウの音ぐらいは聞かせて！
English: So you had better show the judge something! Quick!

Japanese: （ぼくなりの“グウの音” ‥‥それって、やっぱり‥‥）
English: (Slow down! We all know I have a tendency to be wrong more than... I can't even say it!)

Japanese: １つだけ‥‥
English: There's one...

Japanese: １つだけ、気になる 証拠品があります。
English: One piece of evidence that catches my attention.

Japanese: ‥‥まだ、審理されていない 証拠品です！
English: Something that this court has yet to see!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ハッキリ言います。これ以上の 審理の必要性は、感じません。
English: I will say this one more time. I do not feel this trial needs to continue at all.

Japanese: ‥‥一度だけ。 チャンスを与えましょう。
English: However, I am giving you one chance... and only one.

Japanese: お得意のハッタリは 通用しない‥‥そういうことだ。
English: What the judge is saying, Wright, is don't try pulling one of your usual bluffs here.

Japanese: （こいつをまちがえた瞬間 ‥‥すべてが終わる！）
English: (If I mess this up, it's curtains for all of us!)

Japanese: 証拠品を１つだけ、 提示していただきます。
English: You may now present one, and only one piece of evidence.

Japanese: さらに審理を必要とする 重要な証拠品とは、なんですか？
English: Now then, what is this important evidence that you must show to the court?

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンだ、成歩堂。 やはり、ハッタリだったようだな。
English: Sorry, Wright, but this time, your bluffing steered you wrong.

Japanese: その証拠に、 不審な点は見あたりません。
English: I can see nothing strange about this piece of evidence.

Japanese: ま、待ってください！
English: W-Wait!

Japanese: ‥‥ヤクソクは 守っていただきます！
English: I am a man of my word, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: これは‥‥ グラス、ですか？
English: This... is a wine glass, is it not?

Japanese: 現場写真をもう一度、 よく見てください！
English: Please look at the photo of the crime scene one more time!

Japanese: 犯人と被害者が争ったためでしょう ‥‥現場は、荒れています。
English: The scene is a mess because of the victim's struggle against his assailant.

Japanese: 花ビンは割れているし、 化粧品のビンが散乱している‥‥。
English: The vase was broken, his make- up is all over the floor...

Japanese: これらは、おそらくドレッサーの 上に置かれていたものでしょう。
English: These were all things that were at one point, sitting on top of the dresser.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ まあ、そうでしょうね。
English: Hmm... Well, yes, I see your point.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！
English: However!

Japanese: ドレッサーの上の、このグラス だけは、なぜかブジでした。
English: This glass that is sitting on top of the dresser is mysteriously untouched.

Japanese: まっ先に倒れてしまいそうな、 このグラスだけが！
English: The only thing that had not fallen over along with every- thing else is this wine glass!

Japanese: これは、じゅうぶん “不審な証拠”です！
English: This piece of evidence is more than strange enough to warrant further consideration!

Japanese: ど、どうですか？ みなさん‥‥
English: W-Well? What do you all have to say...?

Japanese: あ、いや。たしかにちょっと、 気になりますね‥‥。
English: Ah, well, yes, it is a little peculiar...

Japanese: で、ですよね！　やっぱり！
English: Y-Yes, isn't it!? I thought it was!

Japanese: その、すがるような目は やめてください。
English: You can stop looking at me with those puppy dog eyes of yours now.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事？
English: Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: なんだろうか。
English: What is it, Your Honor?

Japanese: ご意見は‥‥
English: Your opinion...

Japanese: ‥‥意見など、必要あるまい。
English: ...You don't need my opinion.

Japanese: そんなグラス、 なんのイミもないのだから。
English: Because there is no special meaning to that glass.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだと‥‥！
English: ...What!?

Japanese: そんなもの、犯行のあと だれかが置いたと考えればよい。
English: It's safe to say that the glass was set there after the crime took place.

Japanese: たとえば、発見者の華宮　霧緒。
English: By the person who discovered the body, Adrian Andrews, for example.

Japanese: 死体を見ておどろいて、思わず 持っていたグラスを置いたのだ。
English: She could have easily been so shocked that she set the glass down without thinking.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥ありそうなことですね。 どうですか？　弁護人。
English: Hmm... That does sound very plausible. Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （あのグラス‥‥華宮　霧緒が 思わず置いたのか‥‥？）
English: (Could Ms. Andrews really have set that glass down without thinking...?)

Japanese: （たしかに、そうかもしれない）
English: (It's possible that is what happened.)

Japanese: （霧緒さんがアレを置かなかった というコンキョは、ない）
English: (There's nothing that points to Ms. Andrews not being the one who set the cup down.)

Japanese: （異議を申し立てても、木槌で たたきつぶされるだけだ‥‥）
English: (And if I raise an objection here, that would only make the judge slam his gavel down...)

Japanese: ダメ、なるほどくん。
English: You can't think like that, Phoenix.

Japanese: 千尋さん‥‥
English: Mia...

Japanese: あなたは今、ガケっぷちに 小指１本で引っかかっているの。
English: Right now, you're hanging on by a very thin thread.

Japanese: ‥‥どんな小さなことでも、 食いさがっていかないと！
English: Anything else you can grab onto right now is better than nothing!

Japanese: （いきなり、ギリギリまで 追いつめられているわけか‥‥）
English: (So in other words, push as far as we can go...)

Japanese: （ここで弱気なタイドを 見せたら‥‥審理は終了する）
English: (If I appear weak here, the trial is over.)

Japanese: （コンキョなんて、とりあえず あとで見つければいい！）
English: (I can look for my proof later!)

Japanese: （ここはココロの目をつぶって、 この人差し指をつきつける！）
English: (For now, I should trust my instinct and point with certainty!)

Japanese: シビれるわ、なるほどくん。 そのニクらしいほどの開きなおり！
English: They just might fall for it, if you're thought-provoking enough!

Japanese: そんな推測では、 弁護側はナットクできない。
English: The defense would like to challenge the prosecution's theory.

Japanese: 発見者が置いたというのなら、 その証拠を見せてください！
English: We would like to see something that proves it was Ms. Andrews who set the cup on the table!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。いつもと立場が 逆転してしまいましたね。
English: Hmm... You've turned the situation on its head yet again, as usual.

Japanese: 御剣検事。あなたの主張を ウラづける証拠は‥‥？
English: Mr. Edgeworth. Do you have any proof to back up your claim?

Japanese: （あるわけない。デタラメだ！）
English: (There's no way he has any. He's just bluffing!)

Japanese: ‥‥私は弁護人とはちがって、 コンキョのないことは言わない。
English: Unlike Mr. Wright, I never say anything unless I have the evidence to support it.

Japanese: な‥‥ナニっ！
English: Wh-What!?

Japanese: アマく見られたものだな、成歩堂。
English: You're not thinking hard enough today, Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥このグラス。 この私が見逃した、とでも？
English: Did you think this wine glass escaped my notice?

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥
English: Th-Then...

Japanese: もちろん、調べさせたよ。 ‥‥指紋をね。
English: Of course it has been thoroughly inspected... for fingerprints.

Japanese: しもん‥‥
English: Fingerprints...

Japanese: このグラスには、ひとりぶんの 指紋だけ、残されていた。
English: There were only one set of fingerprints left on this wine glass.

Japanese: ひとりぶん‥‥ いったい、だれの！
English: Only one? Well, whose were they!?

Japanese: 被告でも被害者でもない。 ‥‥発見者、華宮　霧緒のものだ。
English: They were not the victim's nor the defendant's. Rather, they were of one Adrian Andrews.

Japanese: なんだってェ！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: 証拠品《グラス》の データを書きなおした。
English: Wine Glass updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: だからこそ私は『発見者が置いた』 と発言したのだ。
English: That is why I said that the person who had discovered the body had left it there.

Japanese: ‥‥おわかりかな？　弁護士クン。
English: Are we done here, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （くそ！ ‥‥またやられたか‥‥！）
English: (Grrr! I can't believe I fell into another trap!)

Japanese: 発見者は、グラスを持って現場の ようすを見に来た。
English: Ms. Andrews was probably holding the glass when she went to see Mr. Corrida.

Japanese: そこに死体を発見した彼女は、 おどろきのあまり、グラスを置く。
English: But upon seeing his dead body, she was stunned, and set the glass down on the dresser.

Japanese: ‥‥そう考えるのが きわめて自然でしょう。
English: Hmm... What you just said makes a lot of sense.

Japanese: クックックッ‥‥弁護人。 少しはリカイできたかな？
English: Tsk tsk tsk... Now do you see, Wright?

Japanese: キミは、私の書いたシナリオを 変えるコトはできないのだよ。
English: You can't change any part of my scenario as it explains everything all too well.

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ぐッ！
English: Grrr!

Japanese: ‥‥私は、この１年間、 “検事”について考え抜いた。
English: I've thought long and hard this past year about what it means to be a "prosecutor".

Japanese: ‥‥そのコタエを、 これから、お目にかけよう。
English: And from here on out, I will show you the answer I have come to discover!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ってください ‥‥御剣検事！
English: W-Wait a second... Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: 私は、検察側の立証は、すでに じゅうぶんだと考えますが‥‥
English: I think the prosecution has provided enough evidence for me to enter my verdict...

Japanese: ‥‥非常にザンネンながら、 そうはいかない。
English: Unfortunately, I can not allow you to pass judgment yet.

Japanese: ワレワレはこれから、さらに 新しい証言を検討するのだ。
English: The prosecution has yet another witness we would like the court to hear from.

Japanese: “新しい証言”‥‥？
English: "Another witness"...?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり。
English: Yes.

Japanese: 係官！　次の証人を 入廷させていただきたい！
English: Bailiff! Please bring in the next witness!

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥いったい、 何を考えているのかしら‥‥。
English: What in the world is Mr. Edgeworth thinking?

Japanese: では、証人。 名前と職業を‥‥
English: Now then, witness, please state your name and occupation...

Japanese: 証人！　名前と職業を明かしてもら
English: Witness! Your name and occupation, please!

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: ぐふうっ！
English: Uurrngh!

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。 イ・チ・コ・ロ‥‥！
English: ...Heh. G.O.T.C.H.A!

Japanese: うむむむむむむむ‥‥
English: Grrrrrrrrr...

Japanese: （さっき、さんざんカッコつけてた のは、なんだったんだ‥‥）
English: (I wonder what happened to that calm composure he had earlier...)

Japanese: もお。ミッちゃんたら！ ‥‥１年ぶりの再会でショ？
English: Oh, Edgey-boy! It's been what, a year since we last met, hasn't it?

Japanese: もっとうれしそうなカオ、 しなさいって！
English: You should be more happy to see me!

Japanese: ‥‥ヘルメットをかぶった写真と 証言は、調書で見ていたが‥‥
English: I saw the report with her testimony, but who knew that under that helmet...

Japanese: まさか、この証人だったとはッ！
English: it was the wicked witch of the witness stand!?

Japanese: オバチャン、わかってる。 わかってるんだからネェ！
English: I tell you, this time I know what I'm supposed to do!

Japanese: いいョ。今日はオバチャン、 なーんでもしゃべっちゃうからサ！
English: So today, I'm going to tell you anything and everything!

Japanese: あることないこと。なーんでも。
English: Even things that don't have to do with that terrible crime.

Japanese: しょ、証人。“あること” だけ、おねがいします。
English: Ms... Witness... "That terrible crime" is all this court needs to know.

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: むふぐッ！
English: Oof!

Japanese: オバチャンは今、 ミッちゃんと話してんだョ。
English: Shush! I'm talking to my dear Edgey-Wedgey right now!

Japanese: ジイちゃんは、引っこんでな！
English: Don't interrupt us, gramps!

Japanese: はあ。
English: Yes, madam.

Japanese: いやいやいやいや、 引っこんでもらっては困る！
English: No no no, please, by all means interrupt her! Please!

Japanese: とにかく証人！ 証言をおねがいする。
English: *ahem* Anyway, witness, your testimony please.

Japanese: セッカチだね若いコはやっぱり。 キライじゃないけどオバチャンも。
English: It's true what they say that youth are hotheaded nowadays. Not that I mind at all, Edgey.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ、 ナニから行っとこうかねェ。
English: Now then, what should I start with...?

Japanese: 証人は事件当夜、現場のホテルで 警備員をしていた。
English: The witness was on security detail at the hotel on the night of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいないだろうか？
English: Is this correct, Ms. Oldbag?

Japanese: イサオちゃんをナマで見られる、 オイシイ仕事だったョ！
English: It was a great job being able to see my dearie Juan!

Japanese: もお、オバチャンの小さなムネも オドるっちゅうハナショ！
English: It was almost too much for my little heart to handle!

Japanese: では、オバ‥‥あなたは、 被害者のファンだったわけですか？
English: You mean... You were a fan of the victim?

Japanese: 世間じゃホラ、オートロが サワヤカでカワユイ、とか、
English: Look, everyone is crazy over that Engarde, saying he's cute in a fresh way, or something.

Japanese: そーんなアマっちょろいコト 言ってるケドねェ。
English: But not me! I wouldn't say anything so silly.

Japanese: あんなオコサマにゃあ、 オバチャン、キュンとこないネ！
English: After all, I have no interest in a little child like him.

Japanese: もお、イサオちゃんヒトスジ だったんだョ、オバチャン！
English: I'm only interested in a real man: Juan Corrida!

Japanese: （いちおう同い年のハズだけど‥‥ イサオさんとオートロさん）
English: (Um... But those two were the same age...)

Japanese: ‥‥ま、そういうワケで、
English: Anyway, as I was saying,

Japanese: あの夜は、ナニかにつけては 控え室の前をウロついてたのョ。
English: I was pacing in front of his room that night.

Japanese: ‥‥では事件当夜、あなたが 目撃したことを聞かせていただく。
English: Very well. Please tell the court what you witnessed the night of the murder.

Japanese: まかせて！　ミッちゃあん！
English: Leave it to me, Edgey-poo!

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- What You Witnessed --

Japanese: とにかく、授賞式のあとも 控え室の前をウロついてたワケ。
English: Anyway, after the ceremony, I went to pace around in the hallway in front of his room.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっとオバチャン、 気になるコトもあったしネェ。
English: There was something I was interested in finding out, you know...

Japanese: ま。シゴトもあったから、ずーっと 見てるワケにも行かなかったケド。
English: Well, since I was on the job, I made sure to keep a good eye out the whole time.

Japanese: ひとりだけ、いたのョ！　イサオ ちゃんの控え室から出てきた男。
English: That's when someone showed up! It was a man coming out of poor Juan's room.

Japanese: 王都楼だョ、王都楼　真悟。 アイツが、コソコソ出てきたんだ！
English: It was Engarde. Matt Engarde. He was trying to sneak his way out of Juan's room!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥被害者の控え室から、 王都楼さんが‥‥
English: Hmm... So Mr. Engarde came out from the victim's room...

Japanese: どうだい、ホレ！　アイツしか いないだろ！　ハンニン。
English: See! It has to be him! He's the murderer!

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I see.

Japanese: では、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Well, Mr. Wright. You may begin your cross- examination.

Japanese: ～目撃したこと～
English: -- What You Witnessed --

Japanese: とにかく、授賞式のあとも 控え室の前をウロついてたワケ。
English: Anyway, after the ceremony, I went to pace around in the hallway in front of his room.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっとオバチャン、 気になるコトもあったしネェ。
English: There was something I was interested in finding out, you know...

Japanese: ま。シゴトもあったから、ずーっと 見てるワケにも行かなかったケド。
English: Well, since I was on the job, I made sure to keep a good eye out the whole time.

Japanese: ひとりだけ、いたのョ！　イサオ ちゃんの控え室から出てきた男。
English: That's when someone showed up! It was a man coming out of poor Juan's room.

Japanese: 王都楼だョ、王都楼　真悟。 アイツが、コソコソ出てきたんだ！
English: It was Engarde. Matt Engarde. He was trying to sneak his way out of Juan's room!

Japanese: なんか、キザなバイクスーツでサ。 ホント、カッコだけはつけてたネ。
English: He was wearing his flashy racing jacket. Honestly, it's all just for show.

Japanese: 思いちがい、カンちがい‥‥でも、 ウソをついてるつもりは、ない。
English: She may not remember things or be mistaken here and there, but I don't think she's lying.

Japanese: タチが悪いですよね。ホント。
English: That's bad for us. Really bad.

Japanese: でも‥‥だから、人間なのよ。
English: But, that's how the human mind is. It also has the tendency to jump off-topic.

Japanese: ‥‥今回も、ちょっと おかしなこと言ってるし。
English: She's strayed onto a few interesting side-topics this time too, hasn't she?

Japanese: カワイイおばさまね。
English: But that's what makes her a sweet old lady, right?

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (That's because you're not the one who has to question her...)

Japanese: オバチャン‥‥証人の 警備担当は、どこだったんですか？
English: Ms. Oldbag, what was your post on that night?

Japanese: ロビー。くだらないショーの ステージを作るハズだったケド。
English: The lobby. I was supposed to help set up the stage for that trifling show.

Japanese: やってらんないわョ。 オボッチャマのステージなんてサ。
English: But I refused to help, I'll have you know. It was for that Lead-headed Samurai's show.

Japanese: オバチャン、ネジ何本か 抜いといてやったし。
English: Heh, I even took out a few of the nails.

Japanese: （中止になってよかったな‥‥ アトラクション・ショー）
English: (Maybe it was a good thing the show didn't go on...)

Japanese: ま。そっちはメガネのマネージャー のコががんばってたみたいだから。
English: Besides, that manager with the glasses seemed to be working hard at it without me.

Japanese: オバチャン、チョコっとだけ ハネを伸ばしてたワケ。
English: So I thought I'd take a break and spread my wings a little.

Japanese: それで、被害者の控え室のあたりで サボってたワケですか‥‥。
English: And that's when you went to hang around the victim's door?

Japanese: “気になること”‥‥ なんですか？
English: Something you were "interested" in? And just what was that?

Japanese: たいしたコトじゃないョ。
English: It's not some little thing I can just go around telling everyone, you know.

Japanese: オバチャンと、イサオちゃん だけのヒ・ミ・ツ。
English: It's top secret, between me and Juan.

Japanese: あ。あと、ミッちゃんね。
English: Ah, and Edgey, of course.

Japanese: 御剣検事。‥‥その “気になること”とは‥‥？
English: Mr. Edgeworth. What is this thing she was "interested" in...?

Japanese: 私は聞いていない。 ‥‥ウワサ話はキライなので。
English: I have no idea. I despise gossip, Your Honor.

Japanese: （“ウワサ話”‥‥）
English: ("Gossip"...?)

Japanese: 事件に関係があるようだったら、 のちほど証言していただこう。
English: If this has something to do with the case, then you can append it to your testimony.

Japanese: ‥‥今は、ムリに聞き出す わけには行かないようね。
English: It looks like we shouldn't force it right now.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥それで、証人は ずっと控え室のあたりに‥‥？
English: Hmm... And did the witness stay in the vicinity of the victim's door the entire time?

Japanese: じゃあ、控え室に出入りした 人間の、正確な数は‥‥
English: Oh? Then would you tell us the number of people who went in and out of Mr. Corrida's room?

Japanese: そんなの知らないョ！
English: I have no idea!

Japanese: オバチャン、数をかぞえるために 生きてるワケじゃないんだからネ！
English: I wasn't born so I could count things for those who didn't pay attention in class!

Japanese: だからオバチャン、自分のトシも ハタチから数えたコトないし！
English: That's why ever since I turned 20, I quit keeping track of how old I really am!

Japanese: ‥‥調書にも書いてませんね。 オソロシイことに。
English: ...Yes, well, that would explain why your age was not recorded in the report.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥
English: In any case...

Japanese: 証人はそもそも、だれかを 目撃したのだろうか？
English: The witness then saw someone, correct?

Japanese: それはいったい、だれですか！
English: Who in the world was that!?

Japanese: そう、せかすんじゃないョ！
English: I'm not allowed to say!

Japanese: こういうネタは、じっくり 引っぱらないと、ソンだからね！
English: This sort of information has to be carefully guarded from the masses, sonny.

Japanese: ‥‥控え室から出てきたオトコ。 それは‥‥なんと‥‥
English: The man that came out of Juan's room... It was... He was...

Japanese: なんと‥‥？
English: Yes...? He was...?

Japanese: ‥‥あ。ダメ。オバチャン、 コワくて言えないッ！
English: Ah! I'm too scared! I can't say his name out loud!

Japanese: （‥‥こんなとき、ぼくに ムチがあったら‥‥）
English: (Oh, what I wouldn't give to have Franziska's whip right about now...)

Japanese: ま。ワカい子をもてあそぶのも ほどほどにしようかネ。
English: Well, I guess I can tell you, since he was such a bad boy anyway.

Japanese: 被告人を見た‥‥ まちがいありませんか！
English: You saw my client!? Are you sure about that!?

Japanese: ないョ。
English: Yessey!

Japanese: 本当に？
English: Really?

Japanese: クドいね！　見たって 言ってんだろ！
English: Annoying brat! When I say I saw someone, I saw that person!

Japanese: （でも、１年前の事件じゃ ものスゴい目にあったからな）
English: (Why do I get a sense of déjà vu?)

Japanese: （‥‥目撃した人物について、 もっと細かく聞いておくか‥‥）
English: (Maybe to avoid a mess like last year, I should delve into this a bit further...)

Japanese: 証人の見た人物のカオについて、 もっと詳しく聞かせてください！
English: Please tell the court about the man's face in more detail!

Japanese: そんなの、聞くまでもないだろ！
English: You don't need me to tell you about his face!

Japanese: アマえるような目に、キザに ニヤけきった口もと！
English: That soft, gentle look in his eyes and his effeminate lips!

Japanese: 右の目にかぶった、サラサラの 髪の毛！　キラリと光る歯！
English: His right eye covered by his silky hair! His sparkling, shining teeth!

Japanese: 歯が‥‥光ったんですか？
English: His teeth were... shining?

Japanese: 今週号のアイツのグラビアじゃ 光ってたョ。
English: Well, he's shining all around in this week's pin-up poster, dearie.
This week's pin-up...? Why do you... I mean!

Japanese: 雑誌のグラビアじゃなくて、 目撃したコトを話してください。
English: I don't care how he looks in this week's issue! Please stay with what you saw that night!

Japanese: おんなじコトだろ？ オートロのカオだったもんネ！
English: What? Engarde's face is the same no matter where it is, right, you whippersnapper!?

Japanese: オバチャンの見た人物‥‥ 何か持っていましたか？
English: What was the man you saw carrying?

Japanese: ‥‥それって、 右手かい？　左手かい？
English: In which hand? His right or his left?
Um...

Japanese: あーあ。マッタク、 コマるんだよネ。
English: Ah, now this is a real mess.

Japanese: オバチャン、イイカゲンな 質問なんか、答えられないョ！
English: I mean, I can't be expected to answer such a vague question!

Japanese: イイカゲンな質問は 困ります、弁護人。
English: Indeed. Please be more specific with your question, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: すす、すみません。
English: S-Sorry...

Japanese: 証人が見た人物ですけど‥‥ 右手に、何か持っていましたか？
English: What was the man you saw carrying in his right hand?

Japanese: いんや。持ってなかったネ。
English: Ah, he wasn't carrying anything in that hand.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、左手は？
English: Then how about his left?

Japanese: 手ブラだったネ。
English: Empty.

Japanese: （ひたすらムナしい）
English: (Well, this whole thing has been a lot of nothing...)

Japanese: ‥‥それで？　成歩堂くん。
English: So, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 今の証言が、 何か重要なのですか？
English: Was this testimony just now important or relevant in any way?

Japanese: そ、そうですね‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥ムダ、 だと思います。
English: I guess it really wasn't that important after all.

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。時間は 大切にしたいものですな。
English: *sigh* Mr. Wright, do you know why we say time is valuable? If not, I suggest you learn.

Japanese: オバチャンの青春、 ３分ほどムダになっちまったネ！
English: You've wasted three minutes of this woman's youth. That's more valuable than gold to me!

Japanese: （ぼくも、２才ほどフケこんだ ような気がする‥‥）
English: (And my intelligence seems to have reverted to that of a toddler...)

Japanese: モチロン、重要だったのです！
English: Of course it was important, Your Honor!

Japanese: 果てしなくつかれる 今の証言の、どこが重要なのか！
English: Then perhaps you would like to point out what part of that testimony was important!

Japanese: ‥‥だって、そう言わなくちゃ 引っこみがつかないから‥‥
English: You don't really have to put it that way to get me to say...

Japanese: 弁護人。人には、メンツより 大切なものがあるのですよ。
English: Mr. Wright. There is something much more valuable than a person's pride.

Japanese: ‥‥それは、時間です。
English: And that is "time".

Japanese: あまりムダな質問を しないようにおねがいします！
English: Please don't waste this court's precious time with worthless questions!

Japanese: ハイ‥‥ （心証を悪くしたか‥‥）
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor... (I think I just wasted his good favor...)

Japanese: 証人の見た人物の服装について、 もっと詳しく聞かせてください！
English: Please tell the court about the man's clothes in more detail!

Japanese: ‥‥メンドくさいコだね マッタク。どうでもいいでショ。
English: What a troublesome man you are. Really, as if something like that matters.

Japanese: よくありません。
English: But it does.

Japanese: えーと、なんだ。 ホレ、アレ。
English: Um... Now what was it... Oh, yes, it was that thing.

Japanese: は？
English: What thing?

Japanese: いっつも着てる、ハデな バイクスーツ。
English: That gaudy thing he's always wearing. That racing jacket.

Japanese: （留置所でも着ていたアレか‥‥）
English: (Ah, he was wearing that at the detention center too...)

Japanese: アレだよ。まったく、 色気づいちゃってサァ‥‥
English: That thing's meant for nothing but seducing women out of their pantaloons! Hmph! Men!!

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: Um, right...

Japanese: ‥‥それで？　成歩堂くん。
English: So, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 今の証言が、 何か重要なのですか？
English: Was this testimony just now important or relevant in any way?

Japanese: そ、そうですね‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: モチロン、重要だったのです！
English: Of course it was important, Your Honor!

Japanese: 果てしなくつかれる 今の証言の、どこが重要なのか！
English: Then perhaps you would like to point out what part of that testimony was important!

Japanese: わからないのか？ 御剣検事‥‥。
English: Don't you see it, Edgeworth?

Japanese: 裁判長！　証人の発言を、 証言に加えてください。
English: Your Honor! I request what the witness said about the jacket be appended to her testimony.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥よくわかりませんが、 証人。おねがいします。
English: Hmm... I don't quite see where you're going with this, but alright. Witness, please...

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ。オバチャン、 ムダ口たたくの、キライなのにサ。
English: Ah well, I don't like to bad- mouth anyone without reason, but if I must...

Japanese: 被告人を見た‥‥ まちがいありませんか！
English: You saw my client!? Are you sure about that!?

Japanese: ないョ。
English: Yessey!

Japanese: 本当に？
English: Really?

Japanese: クドいね！　見たって 言ってんだろ！
English: Annoying brat! When I say I saw someone, I saw that person!

Japanese: （とりあえず、この証言を ゆさぶってもしかたない）
English: (Guess I'm going to have to press a little harder on this one...)

Japanese: （もっと、決定的なムジュンを 探さないとな‥‥）
English: (There's got to be some sort of huge contradiction I can find here...)

Japanese: まちがいなく、被告の バイクスーツでしたか？
English: Are you sure the defendant was wearing a racing jacket?

Japanese: まあネ。
English: What do you think?

Japanese: アイツが他のカッコウをしてるの、 見たコトないからね。
English: It's not like I've seen him in anything other than that horrible thing.

Japanese: きっと、そうだったに ちがいないョ！
English: I'm sure he was wearing it!

Japanese: （自分のカラに 閉じこもってしまった‥‥）
English: (She is so sure of herself that it's to the point of self-absorption...)

Japanese: オバチャン‥‥
English: Ms. Oldbag...

Japanese: なんだい！ 気やすく呼ぶんじゃないョ！
English: What!? Don't say my name for no reason!

Japanese: これ‥‥、 なんだかわかりますか？
English: Do you know what this is?

Japanese: ギャッ！ 忍者ナンジャの第２ボタン。
English: Aaaah! It's button number 2 on the Jammin' Ninja's costume!

Japanese: （さすが、大ファン‥‥ ヒト目で見わけたぞ）
English: (Now I KNOW she's an obsessed fan... She identified it in a single glance!)

Japanese: おくれ！　おくれョ！ ‥‥くれないと、こうだョ！
English: Give it here! Give it here! If you don't give it to me, I'll punish you with this!

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: （うう‥‥血まみれの ボタンをほしがるとは‥‥）
English: (Wow... She really is a die- hard fan to want a button covered in blood...)

Japanese: このボタンは、オートロさんの 身体検査で発見されました。
English: This button was discovered on Mr. Engarde's body during a full body search.

Japanese: そうだろそうだろ！
English: See! See!

Japanese: オートロがやったっていう 何よりの証拠さネ。
English: This button proves beyond a shadow of a doubt it was that rascal Engarde!

Japanese: トノサマンのハカマに 挟まっていたんですよ。
English: It was caught up in the pleats of his Nickel Samurai hakama pants.

Japanese: そうだろそうだろ！
English: See! See!

Japanese: トノサマンといえば オートロだからネ！
English: And Engarde is the Nickel Samurai!

Japanese: ‥‥証人！
English: ...Witness!

Japanese: 今さら説明するのも バカバカしいけど‥‥
English: Now, it may just be me, and I do have an active imagination,

Japanese: さっき、あなたは こう証言したはずです！
English: but just now, didn't you say that the defendant...

Japanese: “目撃した被告人は、 バイクスーツを着ていた”と！
English: Matt Engarde was wearing his "usual racing jacket"!?

Japanese: はあ‥‥カナシイね オバチャンは。
English: Ah... I'm so sorry.

Japanese: ニンゲンを着ているモノで 判断するのかいアンタはッ！
English: Sorry that you judge people based on what they wear!

Japanese: そりゃオバチャンだって、ハヤリの ドレスを着りゃァ、かがやくわョ！
English: If I wore the trendiest dress, then maybe you'd think I was the most gorgeous woman ever!

Japanese: でもそこをグッとガマンして、こん なバカみたいなカッコをしてるワケ
English: But instead, I have to put up with wearing this ridiculous- looking outfit. You'd agree

Japanese: でしょうが！　なんだいこのムネに ぶら下がってるテープレコーダーは
English: this outfit is hideous, right? I've got a tape recorder stuck on my chest! Lemme tell you,

Japanese: 重たくってしょうがないっつーの！ でもコドモたちのユメをこわさない
English: it's HEAVY! So heavy, I wish we would have switched to CDs ages ago! But I'm keeping that

Japanese: ためにオバチャン、エガオでがんば ってるワケョ。だいたいなんだい！
English: dream alive for all those kids out there, I work hard with a smile on my face, don't you

Japanese: アンタのカッコだって、おもしろ味 のカケラもありゃしないちったァ
English: understand!? Now, take a look in the mirror. Your clothes are about as interesting as a

Japanese: ミッちゃんのセンスを見習えっちゅ うハナシだョもうこれでも食らいな
English: documentary on curling! You should take a tip or ten from Edgey-poo. Now HE'S got style!

Japanese: カタカタカタカタ‥‥
English: *rata-tat-tat-tat-tat...*

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！ あ、あなたが見たのは‥‥
English: Now hold your tongue still there for one second! So what you saw in actuality,

Japanese: 被告人・オートロさん自身 ではなく‥‥
English: was not Mr. Engarde, the man...

Japanese: ‥‥トノサマンだった というのですかッ！
English: but Mr. Engarde, the Nickel Samurai!?

Japanese: ‥‥あるいはネ。
English: But when you think about it...

Japanese: どっちだって おんなじコトなのに‥‥
English: They're really one and the same anyway...

Japanese: 証人！　これは重要な コトです！
English: Ms. Oldbag! This is a very important point we're talking about!

Japanese: そうなのかい？ ‥‥ミッちゃん。
English: Edgey-poo! Do you think so too?

Japanese: ‥‥一考の価値はある 問題と言えるかもしれない。
English: Well, it might be something worth thinking about.

Japanese: “重要です”って言えよ！
English: Just say, "It's important" and agree with me for a change!

Japanese: 証人！　よく思い出して 証言してください！
English: Witness! Think carefully and try to remember as much as you can before you testify!

Japanese: はああ。やれやれだョ。
English: *sigh* Alright, if you insist.

Japanese: （こっちが“やれやれ”だョ！）
English: (I should be the one sighing, not you!)

Japanese: ～目撃した人物について～
English: -- Who I Saw --

Japanese: 王都楼、王都楼‥‥あっ！ オバチャン、思い出したョ！
English: Engarde... Engarde... Yes, now I remember!

Japanese: トノサマンだョ、トノサマン。 たしかにそうだったワ！
English: The Nickel Samurai, that's right, it was the Nickel Samurai that I saw!

Japanese: 犯行のときも、着ぐるみのほうが ツゴウがいいだろうしネェ。
English: Yes, it would have been convenient for him to wear his costume during the murder.

Japanese: そのあとすぐ、受賞記念のショーも あったワケだから。
English: He had to go to that post- ceremony stage show right after the crime, you know.

Japanese: イサオちゃんを刺したときから 着てたんだョ、トノサマンの衣装。
English: So he must've worn that Nickel Samurai costume when he was stabbing poor Juan.

Japanese: や、やっぱり‥‥
English: I... I knew it...

Japanese: やっぱり、トノサマン だったんじゃないですか！
English: I knew you'd say he was inside that costume!

Japanese: あらやだアンタ。
English: What?

Japanese: まさか、“トノサマンの中に 他の人間が入っていた”とか、
English: Did you think there could've been someone else inside that costume?

Japanese: そんなフラチなコト 考えてるのかい？
English: Don't be a bad little boy, thinking such rude things.

Japanese: そ、その可能性はあります！
English: But... But the possibility does exist!

Japanese: やれやれ。 ‥‥そんなワケ、ないのにネェ。
English: Ah, young'uns today. I told you, there is no way it was anyone else.

Japanese: ど、どうしてですか？
English: H-How do you know that?

Japanese: だって。 ‥‥そんなワケ、ないから。
English: Because... I said so. And what I say is the truth.

Japanese: （オバチャンに“理由”を聞く ほうがまちがってる、か‥‥）
English: (At least she's just as delightful a witness as she was a year ago...)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Wright. You may cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: ～目撃した人物について～
English: -- Who I Saw --

Japanese: 王都楼、王都楼‥‥あっ！ オバチャン、思い出したョ！
English: Engarde... Engarde... Yes, now I remember!

Japanese: トノサマンだョ、トノサマン。 たしかにそうだったワ！
English: The Nickel Samurai, that's right, it was the Nickel Samurai that I saw!

Japanese: 犯行のときも、着ぐるみのほうが ツゴウがいいだろうしネェ。
English: Yes, it would have been convenient for him to wear his costume during the murder.

Japanese: そのあとすぐ、受賞記念のショーも あったワケだから。
English: He had to go to that post- ceremony stage show right after the crime, you know.

Japanese: イサオちゃんを刺したときから 着てたんだョ、トノサマンの衣装。
English: So he must've worn that Nickel Samurai costume when he was stabbing poor Juan.

Japanese: ううん‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 考える必要なんてないわ。
English: You don't need to think too hard on this one.
Huh?

Japanese: 証言にはムジュンがある。 それはハッキリしてるわ。
English: There's a contradiction in her testimony and it's sitting in plain sight.

Japanese: 問題は、そのムジュンがいったい、 何を意味するのか‥‥？
English: The question is what that contradiction means for us...

Japanese: （‥‥とりあえず、探してみるか、 ムジュン‥‥）
English: (Well, I have to figure out what you're talking about first, but OK...)

Japanese: ‥‥さっさとそれを 証言してください！
English: Would you please get on with your testimony!

Japanese: あ！　なんだいなんだい その言いかたは！
English: Hmph! Watch your language, young man! What sort of tone is that to take with an elder!

Japanese: オバチャンはネェ、ビミョーな おトシごろなんだョ！
English: My youthfulness isn't what it used to be, so you should forgive me for everything!

Japanese: そんなにカミつかれたら、 もお、ヘソ曲げちゃうからネェ！
English: If you keep on barking at me like that, I'll start singing at the top of my lungs!

Japanese: え。
English: Uh, what?

Japanese: なぎさでひとりィ～ トノサマン音頭ォ～‥‥とォ！
English: "A striking figure by the sea, standing all alone is he. He's the Nickel Samurai!"

Japanese: （‥‥歌い出した‥‥）
English: (...She's actually singing... Someone help my poor ears.)

Japanese: 御剣検事。 なんとかしてもらえますか。
English: Mr. Edgeworth. Can you please do something about this racket!?

Japanese: ‥‥証人。証言をおねがいする。 あとでガムをごちそうしよう。
English: Witness. I'll give you a piece of gum later if you'll be good and stick to just the facts.

Japanese: オッケェェェイ！ ‥‥そう来なくちゃネ！
English: OKAAAAAAAY! You promise, right!?

Japanese: 弁護人。ガム代はキサマの 事務所に請求するからな。
English: Wright. I'll be sending the bill for the chewing gum to your office at a later date.

Japanese: （おごれよ、それぐらい‥‥）
English: (Remind me to send you a thank you note later too, Edgeworth, old chum...)

Japanese: ‥‥もっと、自分の証言に セキニンを持ってください！
English: Be a little more careful with your testimony, please!

Japanese: “バイクスーツを着ていた” さっきは、そう証言しましたね！
English: Not too long ago, you said he was wearing his "racing jacket", and now he's not!?
"Not too long ago"...?

Japanese: じゃあ聞こうじゃないか！ アンタ、ちっちゃいコロのユメは？
English: Then let me ask you this! When you were itty-bitty, what was your grand dream?
...Huh?

Japanese: おっきくなったらナニに なりたかったのサ！
English: What did you want to be when you grew up, whippersnapper!?

Japanese: 将来のユメ‥‥ですか。
English: My dream, huh...?

Japanese: み、ミラクル仮面‥‥ でしたけど。それがナニか？
English: Well, I... uh, wanted to be Judge Wackner, hero of the Public's Court. So what!?

Japanese: ホレごらん！
English: See!

Japanese: アンタは今、ミラクル仮面じゃ ないだろうが！
English: And look at where you are now! You're not Judge Wackner, are you!? Are you!?

Japanese: はあ‥‥
English: Well...

Japanese: “ムカシ、ナニを言ったか？”
English: "What I said earlier"...

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコトに、いったい なんの意味があるってのサ！
English: Who puts any weight into things like that!?

Japanese: “今”がすべて！　ムカシのコト なんて、セキニン持てないョ！
English: The "now" is everything! I can't be held responsible for the "past"!

Japanese: （サイバンを根底からくつがえす、 ショーゲキ的な発言だな‥‥）
English: (Since when did court become theatrics over testimonies?)

Japanese: アイツはトノサマンの中に 入ってた。それでいいじゃないか！
English: All that matters is that man was inside that costume. Isn't that enough!?

Japanese: どうしてですか？
English: And why would that be?

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃアンタ。 カオを見られなくてすむし。
English: That way, no one could see his face, of course!

Japanese: でも、オートロさんがトノサマンを 着ても、なんのイミもありません。
English: But there's still no advantage for him that I can see.

Japanese: かえって目立つような 気がしますけど。
English: In fact, you would think the costume would make him stand out all the more!

Japanese: まったく、ニクったらしい子だョ。 ‥‥ああ言えばこう言う‥‥
English: You are such a annoying child, you know that? You disagree with everything I say...

Japanese: （それはオバチャンの方だろ！）
English: (Isn't that what YOU'RE always doing to me!?)

Japanese: アレだョ！　着がえるのが メンドウだったんじゃないかい！
English: I got it! Maybe it was more troublesome for him to change in and out of his costume!

Japanese: ショーには、トノサマンの他に だれが出る予定だったんですか？
English: Was there anyone else scheduled to appear at the post-ceremony show?

Japanese: グランプリのコウホだった連中が 友情出演するはずだったョ。
English: Well, all the contestants were supposed to go onstage in a friendly gesture thing.

Japanese: ‥‥ということは、 忍者ナンジャも‥‥？
English: And that included the Jammin' Ninja...?

Japanese: そういうコトだネ！
English: Of course it included him!

Japanese: だから、イサオちゃんの控え室から オートロが出てきても、
English: That's why when Engarde came out of dear Juan's room,

Japanese: あんまりアヤシイとは 思わなかったワケョ。
English: I didn't give it a second thought.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥なるほど。
English: Hmm... I see.

Japanese: ‥‥ま、とにかく。
English: Well, anyway.

Japanese: 最後にカクニンします。
English: So let me ask you one last time.

Japanese: あなたが見たのは、本当に トノサマンだったのですね？
English: The person you saw, it really was the Nickel Samurai?

Japanese: ナニをカッコつけてんだかね。
English: As showy as ever.

Japanese: オバチャン、最初からずっと そう言ってるだろ？
English: Haven't I been saying that from the very beginning!?

Japanese: （‥‥勝てそうもないな、一生）
English: (Can I throw in the towel yet?)

Japanese: これを見てください。
English: Please take a look at this.

Japanese: なんだい、イミありげに。
English: Yeah, so, it's a knife. Big deal.

Japanese: そんなコトしたってオバチャン、 トキめかないョ！
English: If you're trying to scare me with that, I'll have you know it won't work!

Japanese: い、いやいや‥‥
English: No no, that's not my intention at all...

Japanese: 凶器のナイフ、ですね？ それが、ナニか。
English: That's the knife that was used in the murder, correct?

Japanese: 裁判長。この証拠が重要なのは、 どうしてですか？
English: Your Honor. Do you know why this piece of evidence is important to this case?

Japanese: 聞くまでもありません。 被告の指紋まみれだからでしょう。
English: You don't even have to ask. It's because the defendant's fingerprints are on it.

Japanese: そういうことか‥‥。
English: Is that what you're driving at...?

Japanese: そういうことです。
English: That is exactly what I am driving at.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか？
English: What are we driving at? And whose car are we driving?

Japanese: 犯行の際、トノサマンの 着ぐるみを着ていたのなら‥‥
English: If Mr. Engarde was really in the Nickel Samurai costume at the time of the murder,

Japanese: このナイフに指紋が残る はずがありません！
English: then it's impossible for his fingerprints to have been left on this knife!

Japanese: それどころか、ナイフについていた 指紋も、消えてしまうでしょう。
English: Actually, he would have wiped all previous fingerprints on this knife right off!

Japanese: い、言われてみればそのう‥‥
English: Oh, that's right! The Nickel Samurai wears gloves, doesn't he?

Japanese: 着ぐるみの手袋を脱いでから 刺したのかもしれない！
English: He probably took his gloves off before he began the stabbing!

Japanese: なぜ、そんなことを？ 凶器に指紋を残すためですか？
English: And why would he do something like that? To leave his prints on the murder weapon?

Japanese: ‥‥そんなことは、あり得ない！
English: There is no way he would do something like that!

Japanese: 可能性が１つだけある！
English: However, there is one possibility!

Japanese: その可能性とやらを 聞かせていただきましょう！
English: Then let's hear your "possibility"!

Japanese: カンタンなことだ。
English: It's very simple.

Japanese: 被告人は、トノサマンのかっこうで 被害者の控え室へ行った。
English: The defendant went to the victim's room while in costume as the Nickel Samurai.

Japanese: そのとき、被告人はまだ、 殺意を持っていなかった。
English: At that time, the defendant held no intent to murder.

Japanese: おそらく、受賞記念のショーの 打ち合わせでもしたのだろう。
English: He was probably just going to relax and talk with the victim about the stage show.

Japanese: ‥‥だから、いったん彼は トノサマンの着ぐるみを脱いだ！
English: Which is why he took his gloves off!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥しかし、事件が 起こってしまったわけですね？
English: Hmm... But the murder still did take place...

Japanese: ‥‥被告人と被害者の 仲の悪さは、有名だった。
English: It's well known that the defendant and the victim had bad blood between them.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥それは、 ありそうなことですな。
English: Hmm, yes... I have heard of that...

Japanese: いかがですか？　弁護人。 御剣検事の主張について‥‥。
English: Well, Mr. Wright? What do you have to say about Mr. Edgeworth's theory...?

Japanese: （御剣の主張は、こうだ‥‥）
English: (So let me get this straight. Edgeworth's theory goes like this:)

Japanese: （“被告は、被害者の控え室に 行ったとき、殺意はなかった”）
English: (When the defendant went to the victim's room, he had no intentions of killing him.)

Japanese: （‥‥この主張、今までの御剣の 立証とムジュンしてないか？）
English: (Now, up to this point, are there any problems with his theory...?)

Japanese: （‥‥いや、待て。もう一度 考えてみよう‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I think I need a little more time on this one.)

Japanese: （ダメだ‥‥特にモンダイは 見あたらないぞ‥‥）
English: (Argh... I can't see any real problems with his theory...)

Japanese: ‥‥もし、ここで御剣検事の 主張を認めてしまったら‥‥
English: But if you let Edgeworth's theory stand,

Japanese: また一歩、有罪判決に 大きく近づくわ。
English: then we're one very large step closer to a guilty verdict.

Japanese: 法廷記録をよく見て。 もう一度、考えるの。
English: Look at the Court Record again and take another shot at it.

Japanese: （もう一度、か‥‥）
English: (Yeah, I just have to think about it one more time...)

Japanese: 弁護人。‥‥早く おねがいしますよ。
English: Mr. Wright, please make your decision soon.

Japanese: 今の主張‥‥さっきまでの きみの発言と、ムジュンしている！
English: This theory contradicts something in an earlier testimony!

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥
English: Wh-What are you babbling about...?

Japanese: きみの主張に合わせるため、仮に オートロさんを犯人と考えると、
English: Now, for argument sake, let's suppose Mr. Engarde was the killer. If that's the case,

Japanese: “犯人が控え室を訪れたとき、 殺意がなかった”はずがない！
English: I think it's impossible for the killer to have gone to the victim's room without intent!

Japanese: ‥‥そうやって、イキオイだけで 証拠をつきつけていると‥‥
English: Naturally, you point to the one piece with the least value... but with such vigor!

Japanese: 長生きできないぞ。 人間としても‥‥弁護士としても。
English: Enjoy your energy while you can. Humans don't live forever... Neither do lawyers.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやらまた、 ハッタリだったようですね‥‥。
English: You sure like to think you can bluff your way out of things, don't you?

Japanese: いい？　なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix, pay attention!

Japanese: 仮に王都楼さんが犯人だった、と 考えてみましょう。
English: Let's pretend for a second that Mr. Engarde is the murderer.

Japanese: もし、彼に最初から殺意が なかったとしたら‥‥、
English: Now from that angle, if he didn't have a murderous intent from the very beginning,

Japanese: 現場にあんなものがあるはず、 ないでしょう？
English: then that means there is a certain object that shouldn't be at the crime scene, right?

Japanese: （“あんなもの”‥‥）
English: ("A certain thing"...? What certain thing...?)

Japanese: ‥‥どうかな、弁護士クン。 考えはまとまったかね。
English: How are you doing over there? Busy collecting your thoughts?

Japanese: （もう一度、行ってみるか‥‥ さらにイキオイをつけて）
English: (Alright, let's give this one more try... and with even more spirit this time!)

Japanese: このナイフ‥‥オートロさんが 食事に使っていたものです。
English: This knife. This was used by Mr. Engarde at dinner.

Japanese: そ、そのとおりですね。
English: Y-Yes, we did establish that.

Japanese: つまり、もしオートロさんが 犯人なら‥‥、
English: Which means that if my client was, in fact, the killer...

Japanese: このナイフを持って、イサオさんの 控え室を訪ねたことになります。
English: Then he brought this knife with him when he went to visit Mr. Corrida.

Japanese: ‥‥そうなるな‥‥
English: I suppose...

Japanese: しかし‥‥ きみはさっき、こう言った。
English: However, you just said it yourself.

Japanese: “被害者の控え室に行ったとき、 オートロさんに殺意はなかった”
English: "At that time, the defendant held no intent to murder."

Japanese: それならば！　ナイフなど 持っていくはずがない！
English: If that were true, then why would he bring a knife!? He wouldn't, would he!?
Hmm...

Japanese: つまり、御剣検事！
English: Which means, Mr. Edgeworth,

Japanese: きみの主張は、最初から ムジュンしていたんだよ！
English: your theory was flawed from supposition one!

Japanese: そして、さらにもうひとつ！
English: And one more thing!

Japanese: 犯行の瞬間、もし犯人が着ぐるみを 着ていたのならば‥‥、
English: If the murderer was wearing the costume at the time of the murder...

Japanese: ナイフには、衣装の手ぶくろの あとがつきます！
English: Then there should be glove marks left on the knife!

Japanese: 当然、王都楼さんの指紋だけが べたべた残っているはずがない！
English: Which means the defendant's fingerprints shouldn't be all over it like bees on a hive!

Japanese: つまり、あのナイフは‥‥
English: And that brings me to my final point...

Japanese: 真犯人によって偽装された、 ニセの証拠だったのです！
English: This knife was planted by the real killer to hide their identity and mislead us!

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛に！ ‥‥静まりなさいッ！
English: O-Order! Order, I say! Order in the court!!

Japanese: このナイフが‥‥ニセの証拠！
English: Was this knife really planted by the killer!?

Japanese: いったい‥‥犯人は なんのために、そんなことをッ！
English: Why would the murderer do such a thing!?

Japanese: モチロン、犯行方法を ごまかすためです！
English: It's to hide the way in which the murder was carried out, of course!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: M-More of your nonsense!?

Japanese: 解剖記録を見てください！
English: Take another good look at the autopsy report!

Japanese: 被害者は、じつはマフラーで 絞殺されているのです！
English: The victim actually died from strangulation!

Japanese: 犯人は、これをかくすため、 ナイフでムネを刺したのです！
English: The killer tried to hide this by stabbing a knife into the victim's chest!

Japanese: しかし‥‥解剖の結果、絞殺された ジジツはすぐ、判明しましたよ？
English: But doesn't the autopsy admit that the cause of death was strangulation?

Japanese: まあ‥‥そうですケド。
English: Well, yes, the real cause of death was easily discovered...

Japanese: ゼンゼンごまかせて ないではないかッ！
English: Then I'd say the knife did a terrible job of "concealing" this fact!

Japanese: ははは。
English: Aha ha ha...

Japanese: その笑いも、ゼンゼン ごまかせてないッ！
English: And that laugh doesn't do a very good job of concealing your error either!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！ ‥‥もう一度、聞きますよ！
English: Mr. Wright! I will ask you one more time!

Japanese: モチロン、オートロさんに 罪を着せるためですよ！
English: It's to frame my client, Mr. Engarde, of course!

Japanese: 罪を‥‥
English: To frame...

Japanese: そんなもの‥‥ コジツケにすぎないッ！
English: A-Aren't you forcing the interpretation just a little too hard on this one!?

Japanese: しかし、証人の目撃した犯人が “トノサマン”である以上‥‥、
English: But we just established that the witness saw the "Nickel Samurai" in costume,

Japanese: 凶器のナイフにオートロさんの 指紋が残ることは、あり得ない！
English: and if that were true, then there shouldn't be a single fingerprint on this knife!

Japanese: ぐぐうううウウッ！ 証人ンンンンンッ！
English: Grrrrrrrrr! Witneeeeeesssssss!!

Japanese: なんかまた、アンタにメイワク かけちゃったみたいだねェ。
English: Looks like I've made your life a tiny bit more difficult, huh Edgey...?

Japanese: ううううう‥‥
English: Gnnnnngh...

Japanese: ‥‥証人。あなたはホントに 見たんですか？　トノサマンを。
English: Witness, did you or did you not really see the Nickel Samurai?

Japanese: そ、そりゃネ。たしかに、 最初は忘れてたけどサ‥‥
English: Well, I guess at first I might have forgotten, but...

Japanese: それにしても、トノサマンと オートロさんをまちがえるなんて！
English: Are you saying you mixed up Mr. Engarde with the Nickel Samurai, his character on TV!?

Japanese: だって、しょーがないでしょッ！
English: But I mean, I can't really do anything about that!

Japanese: オバチャンが控え室のあたりを こそこそウロついていたのは‥‥
English: Look, I was waiting around in front of their doors because, well...

Japanese: トノサマンが出てくるのを 待ってたワケじゃないからネェ。
English: Well, I wasn't waiting around for the Nickel Samurai, that's for sure!

Japanese: （“トノサマンが”出てくるのを 待っていたワケじゃなかった？）
English: (She wasn't waiting for the "Nickel Samurai"...?)

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥いったい、だれが 出てくるのを待ってたんですか？
English: Alright then... Who were you waiting around for then?

Japanese: ‥‥フン！　警備上のヒミツだョ！
English: Hmph! That's top secret to anyone outside of security!

Japanese: それはもう、聞くまでもなく 藤見野　イサオさんだったのでは？
English: I have a feeling that you were waiting for Mr. Juan Corrida. Am I correct, witness?

Japanese: ‥‥フッフッフッ‥‥
English: Ha ha ha.

Japanese: そう考えるのが、底のアサい シロートの悲しさ、ってヤツだネ。
English: The way you think, you are a sad amateur with a terrible case of near-sightedness.

Japanese: シロート‥‥
English: Amateur...? Me...? What am I an amateur of...?

Japanese: （オバチャンは‥‥被害者の 控え室の前をウロついていた）
English: (So Oldbag was waiting around in front of the victim's room...)

Japanese: （しかし、イサオさんが目当て だったワケではないらしい‥‥）
English: (But it doesn't sound like she was waiting to catch a glimpse of Mr. Corrida...)

Japanese: もしかして‥‥なるほどくん！ あのおばさまが待っていたのは‥‥
English: Maybe... Phoenix! Maybe the old bag was waiting around for "that" person!

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、考えれば わかることらしいな‥‥）
English: (Hmm, if it's who I think Mia's hinting at... It's certainly possible...)

Japanese: 証人。
English: Ms. Oldbag.

Japanese: あなたは、この人物が出てくるのを 待っていたのではないですか？
English: You were waiting for this person to come out of the victim's room, weren't you?

Japanese: ナニやってるの！ なるほどくん！
English: What are you talking about, Phoenix!?

Japanese: そんなヒト、死ぬまで待っても 出てこないでしょ！
English: She could've waited until the next century and this would never have appeared!

Japanese: （うう‥‥おでこを ピシャリとやられた‥‥）
English: (Ouch... Busted... Guess there's no fooling her.)

Japanese: コラ、そこ！ ‥‥いちゃいちゃしない！
English: You two! What do you think you're doing flirting in my courtroom!?

Japanese: してませんよ‥‥
English: We weren't doing anything like that... Honest...

Japanese: （とにかく、もう一度 考えてみよう‥‥）
English: (Well, back to the drawing board on this one...)

Japanese: （オバチャンは‥‥被害者の 控え室の前をウロついていた）
English: (Oldbag was waiting around in front of the victim's room...)

Japanese: （しかし、イサオさんが目当て だったワケではないらしい‥‥）
English: (But it doesn't sound like she was waiting to catch a glimpse of Mr. Corrida...)

Japanese: このかたは‥‥
English: Who is this person...?

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒‥‥ 王都楼　真悟のマネージャーです。
English: This is Adrian Andrews, Mr. Engarde's manager.

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥どうして、 被告のマネージャーが、被害者と？
English: B-But why would the defendant's manager be in the victim's room...?

Japanese: 最近、世間では こんなウワサが流れているらしい。
English: It seems that this is the latest rumor in circulation, Your Honor.

Japanese: ‥‥ほほお。これはこれは。 ふむふむ‥‥ははあ‥‥なんとも。
English: ...Hmm... Oh... This is... Well, this is... Hmm, hmm... Hah, I see...

Japanese: （裁判長‥‥記事に ボットーしている‥‥）
English: (The judge seems to be really into the "article"... If it can be called such a thing...)

Japanese: では、この“マネージャーの Ｋ．Ｋ嬢”というのが‥‥？
English: Then this manager with the initials A. A.... Are you saying it's...?

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒‥‥少なくとも 証人は、そう考えたのだろう。
English: Adrian Andrews. Without a doubt, the witness thought so as well.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥フッ。 バレちゃァしょうがないネェ。
English: Hmph. Looks like you found me out.

Japanese: いいョ。オバチャンのゴクヒ任務、 ぶちまけちゃおうかネ。
English: Well, that's fine. I can throw away this whole sworn to confidentiality stuff.

Japanese: しょ、証人‥‥？ あなたはいったい‥‥
English: W-Witness...? What in the world are you...

Japanese: ‥‥不吉ね。 これはもう、かぎりなく不吉よ。
English: Watch out, Phoenix. I've got a bad feeling about this... A very bad feeling.

Japanese: オバチャン、ある情報を 入手していたのョ。‥‥ゴクヒに。
English: I got some information... Some very secret information from a certain source.

Japanese: ‥‥だから、そいつを 調査してたワケ。‥‥ゴクヒで。
English: So that's why I was doing my own little investigation... In secret, of course.

Japanese: な、なんのために‥‥？
English: B-But what for...?

Japanese: 自己形成ョ。‥‥ゴクヒの。
English: Oh, just for myself... Personal reasons and all that.

Japanese: どうしますか？　御剣検事。
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth. How will you proceed from here?

Japanese: まったく気が進まないが‥‥ 聞かないわけにもいくまい。
English: I really don't want to do this, however I cannot simply let this point slide.

Japanese: わかりました。
English: I see.

Japanese: では、証人。その、ゴクヒ情報に ついて、証言してください。
English: Very well then. Witness, please testify about this "secret information".

Japanese: ‥‥カクゴするんだねェ‥‥ アンタたちッ！
English: Get ready! This is going to take the wind out of you young'uns!

Japanese: （ダレのことだ？ ‥‥“アンタたち”って）
English: (I'm sure we're all capable of handling this... Really, it's not like we're ten years old.)

Japanese: ～ゴクヒの情報～
English: -- Secret Information --

Japanese: そこのオートロったらサ、 ひでェオトコなんだョ！
English: That Engarde is one evil, evil man!

Japanese: イサオちゃんを、スキャンダルで ダメにしよう、なんて考えてサ！
English: He thought he could ruin poor Juan by causing a huge scandal!

Japanese: そのために、自分のマネージャーを イサオちゃんにくっつけたんだョ！
English: So to do that, he sent his own manager to get in close with Juan!

Japanese: ここはひとつ、オバチャンが 立ち上がるしかないでショ！
English: I cannot condone such dirty tricks! So I took action!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。この話、ゴクヒだョ。 まだ、だれも知らないんだからネ。
English: ...Oh, and this is top secret, you got that!? Nobody else but you and me know yet, OK?

Japanese: 被告人が、マネージャーを‥‥？ なんか、シラジラしい話ですねえ。
English: The defendant sent his manager...? What a distasteful topic for this court!
