Japanese: なんだい！ エラいヒトも言ってるだろ！
English: What!? Nobody's above gossip!

Japanese: “真実とは、ややシラジラしい モノである”ってサ！
English: And isn't there a saying!? "The truth is never pleasant."

Japanese: （聞いたことないぞ‥‥）
English: (Never heard that one before...)

Japanese: 御剣検事。 華宮　霧緒さんのことは‥‥？
English: Mr. Edgeworth. What about this Adrian Andrews person...?

Japanese: いちおう調査はしているが‥‥
English: We have looked into this matter,

Japanese: この記事がジジツであるという 確証は、存在しない。
English: and found that the "truth" the article proposes is, in fact, baseless gossip.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥しかし、もしこれが 本当ならば‥‥
English: Hmm... But should this be true...

Japanese: 被告人の、被害者に対する 悪意を立証する証拠になりますな。
English: Then this proves that the defendant did bear ill-will towards the victim.

Japanese: （‥‥あのウワサを ぶちコワす必要があるわけか）
English: (...So this means I have to smash this rumor once and for all.)

Japanese: ‥‥では、成歩堂くん。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Now then, Mr. Wright. You may cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: 気をつけて。おばさまは今、 かなりエキサイトしてるわ。
English: Be careful. The old bag seems rather excited right now.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだョ！　クソヤローなんだ あのオートロってヤツァ！
English: That's right! Engarde is nothing but your average foul-blooded youth!

Japanese: （あのオバチャンに 対抗するためには‥‥）
English: (Well, as the old saying goes... You've gotta burn old bags with fire!)

Japanese: （こっちもエキサイトして ぶつかるしかない‥‥！）
English: (Time to fire up the afterburners and hit the highway to the danger zone!)

Japanese: ～ゴクヒの情報～
English: -- Secret Information --

Japanese: そこのオートロったらサ、 ひでェオトコなんだョ！
English: That Engarde is one evil, evil man!

Japanese: イサオちゃんを、スキャンダルで ダメにしよう、なんて考えてサ！
English: He thought he could ruin poor Juan by causing a huge scandal!

Japanese: そのために、自分のマネージャーを イサオちゃんにくっつけたんだョ！
English: So to do that, he sent his own manager to get in close with Juan!

Japanese: ここはひとつ、オバチャンが 立ち上がるしかないでショ！
English: I cannot condone such dirty tricks! So I took action!

Japanese: ‥‥あ。この話、ゴクヒだョ。 まだ、だれも知らないんだからネ。
English: ...Oh, and this is top secret, you got that!? Nobody else but you and me know yet, OK?

Japanese: なんてことないウワサ話 ‥‥ですよね。
English: Sounds like just another tidbit of gossip, doesn't it?

Japanese: でも、ほっておいちゃダメよ。 依頼人の印象が悪くなるわ。
English: But you can't just ignore it either. It makes your client look bad either way.

Japanese: （特にムジュンした 証言とは思えない‥‥）
English: (Well, I don't think I heard anything really out of the ordinary just now...)

Japanese: でも‥‥何かが引っかかるわ。
English: There has to be something we can catch her on...

Japanese: （たしかに。 そこを追求してみるか‥‥）
English: (Yeah... And when I find it, I'm going to press the heck out of it...)

Japanese: コンキョもなく、そんなコトを 言われては困ります！
English: You can't say something like that without proof! That's just slander!

Japanese: あの女が、イサオちゃんに 近づいていたのはジジツでショ！
English: But it's true! That woman was getting intimate with poor Juan!

Japanese: ホレ！　書いてあるじゃないのサ！ “マネージャーのＫ．Ｋ嬢”って。
English: Look! It says so right here, doesn't it!? "Manager to the stars, Ms. A. A.!"

Japanese: ‥‥でも、その雑誌、タイトルが 《ゴシップランド》ですよ？
English: But the name of the magazine this came from is "Gossip Land"...

Japanese: なんだい？　“ゴシップ”は １つ残らずウソだってのかい？
English: What? Are you saying that "gossip" is all just a pack of lies?

Japanese: じゃア、なんだ。
English: Hmph, what do you know?

Japanese: “ニュース”は１つ残らずホントの コトだと、アンタ誓えるのかい！
English: I suppose next you'll swear to me that the "news" is 100% truth!

Japanese: え。
English: Um...

Japanese: マッタク。ニュースとゴシップ、 サベツするんじゃないョ！
English: Honestly, sonny. You can't discriminate between the news and gossip!

Japanese: サベツはよくありませんね、 弁護人。
English: Yes, discrimination is bad, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （サベツしてたのか‥‥？　ぼく）
English: (Discriminate? When did I do anything like that?)

Japanese: とにかく、ユルせないのは オートロだョ、オートロ！
English: Anyway, Engarde will never get me to say "touché"!

Japanese: スキャンダル‥‥というのは？
English: A scandal...? What do you mean by that?

Japanese: ヤダネェこのコは。 スキャンダルも知らないのかい？
English: You're a dimwitted one, aren't you? I can't believe you don't know what a "scandal" is!

Japanese: マッタク、最近の中学校じゃ ナニを教えてるんだろうネ‥‥
English: Honestly, what are they teaching kids in middle school these days...?

Japanese: いやいや。“スキャンダル”の イミを聞いてるんじゃないですよ！
English: Ah, no no! I wasn't asking what the word "scandal" means! Even I know that much!

Japanese: オートロはね！　人気を ひとりじめしようとしたんだョ！
English: Well! That Engarde thought he could own a monopoly on popularity!

Japanese: オートロさんがそんなことを したという証拠はありません！
English: You don't have any proof that Mr. Engarde did any such thing!

Japanese: ‥‥さっきから聞いてりゃ ショーコ、ショーコってさァ。
English: You must be suffering from shock; the shock of hearing the truth.

Japanese: アンタ、ショーコがなきゃ なんにもできないのかい！
English: And now, since you're in so much shock, you can't do anything right!

Japanese: （なんにもできないんだけど‥‥）
English: (You're right, I can't do any- thing, but boy do I wish I could do something about you.)

Japanese: じゃあ、聞こうじゃないか！
English: Alright then, sonny, show me what you've got!

Japanese: オートロが、イサオちゃんに悪さを しなかった証拠はあるのかい！
English: Can you show me proof that Engarde didn't bear any ill- will towards Juan!?

Japanese: ‥‥証拠はありません。
English: ...I don't have anything to offer.

Japanese: ほーれ、見たことか。
English: Seeeeeee! Just as I thought.

Japanese: いいかい、言うだけなら タダなんだ！
English: And you were lecturing me about saying things without proof!

Japanese: オバチャン、言いたいほうだい いかせてもらいますよォ！
English: You've just given me a free pass to say whatever I want whenever I want, silly boy!

Japanese: （‥‥まいったな‥‥）
English: (Me and my big mouth...)

Japanese: ま、そういうワケだからサ。
English: That's the way the cookie crumbles. For you, anyway.

Japanese: ここで引っこんだら オトコじゃありません！
English: I'm not going to let you push me around that easily!

Japanese: そう言って死んでいったヤツを 何人も知ってるョ。
English: Heh. There's many a man who've said those exact words and paid dearly for them.

Japanese: では、お見せしましょう‥‥。
English: Maybe you should brace yourself this time!

Japanese: オートロさんが、被害者に悪さを しなかった証拠は‥‥！
English: Here is my proof that Mr. Engarde did not hold any ill-will towards the victim!

Japanese: ‥‥ヤレヤレ。 ミッちゃん、出番だョ。
English: ...Is that it? You can take it from here, Edgey-poo!

Japanese: え！　な、なんだろうか。
English: EH!? Wh-What are you handing it over to me for!?

Japanese: 言ってやっておくれョ。 このギザギザのニイちゃんに。
English: I want you to give that jagged-headed baboon-boy a message for me! Please?

Japanese: カッとなっても、モノゴトは うまくいかない、ってサ。
English: Tell him that no matter what he does, the only thing he's good at is failing.

Japanese: ‥‥ま。そういうコトだ、 成歩堂　龍一。
English: Um... What she said, Wright! Or should I say... Baboon-boy!

Japanese: たしかに、それはなんの 証拠にもなりませんね。
English: Yes, I agree this isn't much of a piece of evidence... Baboon-boy.

Japanese: （シッパイしたらしいな‥‥）
English: (Did I just gain a new nickname here...?)

Japanese: “立ち上がる”というのは‥‥？
English: So, what do you mean by "I took action"...?

Japanese: だからサ。決定的なゲンバを おさえてやるワケョ！
English: Like I already told you, I was lying in wait close to the crime scene!

Japanese: あのフラチな女が、イサオちゃんの 控え室から出てきたら‥‥
English: Once that slimy woman came out of Juan's room...

Japanese: とっつかまえて、 おしおきョ、おしおき。
English: I was going to capture her, and teach her a good lesson. Something you young'uns need!

Japanese: おしおき‥‥？
English: You were going to "teach her a good lesson"...?

Japanese: この、カラダにワルそうな ビーム、食らわしてやるのサ！
English: I was going to make her eat the damaging beams of my ray gun!

Japanese: ‥‥こんなふうにッ！
English: Like this!

Japanese: や、やめなさいッ！
English: N-No, stop!

Japanese: ‥‥ま。ケッキョク あの女は出てこなかったけどネ。
English: Well, it was too bad that woman didn't come through the door that night.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください。
English: Wait!

Japanese: なんだい！ オバチャン、いそがしいんだョ！
English: What!? I'm a busy woman! Tea time with the kids is over!

Japanese: だれも知らない、ゴクヒ情報‥‥
English: Secret information that no one else knows yet...

Japanese: どうしてオバチャンが 知ってるんですか！
English: If that's true, then how do you know this "secret information"!?

Japanese: え！　そ、そりゃアンタ‥‥ オバチャン、プロだし‥‥
English: Huh!? Well... That's... because I'm a pro... Yes, that's it...

Japanese: ひ、ヒミツだョ！　そんな目で 見たって、教えるもんかいッ！
English: I-It's a secret! Even if you drill a hole into my brain, you'll never find out!

Japanese: （オバチャンが、あの情報を どこから入手したのか‥‥？）
English: (How in the world did that old bat get such a "secret" piece of information...?)

Japanese: （‥‥もう少し、考えを まとめたほうがいいな‥‥）
English: (I should probably think a bit more about this... I don't want to do anything rash...)

Japanese: （とりあえず、今は ようすを見ておこう）
English: (For now, maybe I should sit back and watch how things play out...)

Japanese: オバチャンが、どこから 情報を入手したか‥‥
English: If you don't be a good girl and tell me where you got this secret information...

Japanese: 言わなきゃ、いつまでたっても 帰れませんよッ！
English: You won't get to go home today!

Japanese: うう‥‥
English: Umm...

Japanese: コラ！　ナニやってんの ミッちゃん！　出番だョ！
English: Edgey-boy! What are you doing!? Help me!

Japanese: え！　な、なんだろうか。
English: Eh!? What do I have to do with this!?

Japanese: シャキーンとキメてやってョ。 ‥‥オバチャンのためにサ。
English: Just do this for me... You'll get your reward.

Japanese: ふう‥‥やむを得ないな。
English: Hmph. I don't want your reward, but you don't leave me much of a choice...

Japanese: 情報など、 どこからでも入ってくる！
English: The witness could have gotten her information from anywhere!

Japanese: 証人のアタマの中に、突然わき出す ことだってあるだろう！
English: It's even possible that it was spontaneously made up inside the witness' head!

Japanese: （そ、それは“出まかせ”と いうのでは‥‥）
English: (And he lectures ME on "reckless blabbering"!?)

Japanese: 情報の出どころがハッキリ しないかぎり‥‥
English: As long as we don't know where this information came from,

Japanese: この尋問は、 ムダのようですな。
English: it's quite a waste of time to focus on this line of questioning.

Japanese: （オバチャンがどこから 情報を手に入れたか‥‥）
English: (Where did Oldbag get her inside information...?)

Japanese: （そいつを立証できる 証拠は、ないのか‥‥？）
English: (There has to be something I can use to figure out it out!)

Japanese: ‥‥だれも知らない “ゴクヒ情報”‥‥
English: So no one else is supposed to know this "secret information" correct?

Japanese: それを、なぜ証人が 知っていたか‥‥？
English: If that's true, then why do you know it, Ms. Oldbag...?

Japanese: ななな、なんだいその、ヒトを あわれむような目はッ！
English: Wh-Wh-Why are you looking at me like that...? Stop that!

Japanese: 証人！
English: Witness!

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、コイツで 情報を知ったのです！
English: I'm sad to say it, but this is how you found out this secret, isn't it!?

Japanese: キサマの、“証拠”と称する 悪意あるシロモノ‥‥
English: You call that "proof"!? It looks more like proof of spite towards the witness!

Japanese: “ウワサ話”と同じく、 なんのコンキョもないッ！
English: If you have nothing to back up your claims, then they are no more valid than "gossip"!

Japanese: ‥‥異議を認めます。
English: Objection sustained.

Japanese: 証人は、弁護人の悪意ある尋問に 屈せず、証言をつづけてください。
English: Witness, please feel free to ignore the defense's gesture of ill-will and continue.

Japanese: あいョ。 ‥‥オバチャン、負けないからネ。
English: Okie-dokie! See, nothing can get me down!

Japanese: （ワルモノにされてしまった‥‥）
English: (Great... That did nothing but set me back a few minutes of my life...)

Japanese: 自称ジャーナリストの 大沢木　ナツミさん‥‥
English: The "investigative photographer", Lotta Hart...

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。あの、やんちゃな ムスメさんですか。
English: Oh yes... I remember that mischievous girl.

Japanese: 彼女のメモが、事件当夜‥‥ 紛失しているんです。
English: She reported that she had lost a certain note she had written to herself.

Japanese: 紛失‥‥？
English: She reported such a thing...?

Japanese: そのメモには、彼女の妄‥‥ 想像が記されていました。
English: On that piece of paper, she had written down some of her outrageo... er, impressions

Japanese: ‥‥被害者と、華宮　霧緒の 関係について。
English: about the relationship between the victim and Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: な‥‥ナニィィッ！ 妄想だってェェェッ！
English: Wh-What!? Outrageous ideas, you say!?

Japanese: いやいや。想像です。
English: No no no, I said impressions.

Japanese: じゃあ‥‥じゃあ‥‥！
English: Then... Then...!

Japanese: この紙ッきれ、なんのイミも なかったってのかいッ！
English: Then everything written on this piece of paper is completely meaningless!

Japanese: あっ！　そのメモ！
English: Ah! That's it! That's the note!
Ah!

Japanese: あややややややややややややや、 こ、これはその、ちがうんだョ！
English: AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHHHHHH! Noooo! You see, this is some- thing completely different!

Japanese: これ、オバチャンの、ゴクヒの お買い物メモなんだョォォォォッ！
English: This is my top secret list of groceries to buyyyyyyy!!

Japanese: ‥‥では、やはりあなたは、 ナツミさんのメモを真に受けて？
English: Hmm, then you are the one who took Ms. Hart's note?

Japanese: オバチャン、イサオちゃんの 大ファンだったんだ！
English: I'm a huuuuuuuge fan of Juan's, that's why!

Japanese: あのモジャモジャアタマの ハレンチなムスメが‥‥
English: That infamous, puffy-haired whippersnapper...

Japanese: “オートロのインボーやッ！” って‥‥
English: She's working with that evil Engarde! She said so herself! "En garde! I'm his sidekick!"

Japanese: それはそれはウレシそうに ハシャいでやがったからサ。
English: She was so happy, smiling like a silly duck.

Japanese: ちょっと、ナニを書いてるのか チェックしてやったワケ。
English: I was only checking what she had written!

Japanese: 信じておくれよォォ！ ミッちゃああん！
English: Edgey-poo! You believe me, don't you!?

Japanese: うぐ。
English: Nngh...

Japanese: ちょっとした、オトメの おちゃめなデキゴコロなんだョゥ！
English: I was only trying to help out like the angel I am!

Japanese: ヒトサマのモノに手を出したの、 この紙ッきれだけなんだョゥゥゥ！
English: It's only one little piece of paper! I've never taken anything else before!

Japanese: その、茶色い猫ナデ声は やめていただきたい。
English: You really should come with a supply of cheese to match your vintage whine.

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、メモの１枚ぐらいなら、 大目に見てあげましょうか。
English: Well, it was only a piece of paper. I suppose we can overlook this just this once.

Japanese: （オバチャン、かなり ハンセイしてるみたいだし‥‥）
English: (She looks like she's really sorry...)

Japanese: （このへんで カンベンしておくか？）
English: (Should I forgive her?)

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、このへんで カンベンしてあげましょう。
English: I guess we should forgive her.

Japanese: ケッ！　だれがアンタなんかに カンベンしてくれってたのんだョ！
English: Hmph! Who the heck are you to decide who should be forgiven, you delinquent!

Japanese: ‥‥ぐ。 （かわいげのないヒトだな‥‥）
English: Grr... (Number of endearing qualities: ZERO.)

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Phoenix.

Japanese: あのおばさまを あまやかしてはダメ。
English: You can't go easy on her. She's no frail old lady.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥
English: Y-Yeah...

Japanese: ‥‥カスカスになるまで しぼりあげてやるの！
English: You should be pressing her until she gives in!

Japanese: （千尋さんも‥‥コワい‥‥）
English: (Mia is really scary today... *gulp*)

Japanese: （ここで、つけ上がらせては ダメだ‥‥）
English: (If I let up on her now, she'll get away...)

Japanese: （決定的なダメージを 与えてやらないとな！）
English: (I have to find some way to inflict a deafening blow to the prosecution!)

Japanese: ‥‥証人！
English: Witness!

Japanese: “ヒトのものを盗んだのは、 そのメモだけ”‥‥
English: You said that the only thing you stole was that note.

Japanese: そう言いましたね？
English: Is this correct?

Japanese: ぬぬぬ、“盗んだ”だってェ！ ヒトのハナシ、ナニ聞いてんのサ！
English: S-S-Stole!? Why don't you listen more carefully, you annoying brat!?

Japanese: この紙クズ、捨てにいく手間を はぶいてやっただけでショ！
English: I saved this piece of paper from the terrible, lonely trash can! That's all!

Japanese: ‥‥オバチャン！ あなたはウソをついている！
English: You're lying, dammit! And I can prove it!!

Japanese: まま、またそうやって、 オバチャンの社会的信用を‥‥
English: Are you putting my credibility under scrutiny again!?

Japanese: ‥‥証人ッ！
English: Ms. Oldbag!

Japanese: あなたが盗んだのは、 メモだけではないはずです！
English: I don't believe that the note is the only thing you stole that night!

Japanese: フッ‥‥証人の社会的信用より、
English: Hmph. More than the witness' credibility,

Japanese: キサマの社会的立場が 音を立てて崩れ落ちたようだな。
English: perhaps you should be worrying about your legal standing, or should I say, falling?
Um...

Japanese: これから先、あなたの異議は ボリュームをしぼって聞きます。
English: I'm setting the volume down to low so I won't have to waste my time with your objections!

Japanese: （なんてこった‥‥ 補聴器を使ってたのか、裁判長）
English: (Hmm... I never knew the judge had hearing aids...)

Japanese: （くそッ！　もう一度だ）
English: (Argh! Now's not the time to be wondering about health conditions! OK, one more try!)

Japanese: ‥‥オバチャン！
English: Ms. Oldbag!

Japanese: そのメモ‥‥カメラのケースに 入っていたはずです！
English: That note was with a camera, inside its case, wasn't it?

Japanese: カメラ‥‥
English: A camera...?

Japanese: きのう、ナツミさんが 大さわぎしていました。
English: Yesterday, Lotta Hart was raising a huge stink over her camera.

Japanese: “定価１６万円のカメラが 消えた”‥‥と！
English: She kept saying something like, "My sweetie $1,600 camera disappeared on me!"

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥まさか、証人！
English: Why... Why... Witness!!

Japanese: なんだい。
English: What is it, gramps?

Japanese: メモがあるということは、当然！ カメラも持っているはずです！
English: If you have the note, then it is only logical that you have the camera too!

Japanese: クッ‥‥ バレちゃアしょうがないねェ‥‥
English: Grr... Looks like you found me out again, sonny...

Japanese: アンタの探してるカメラ‥‥ コレのことかい。
English: Is this the camera you're looking for?

Japanese: あ。
English: Ah, that's...!

Japanese: なにサ！　こんなカッコしてるけど オバチャンだって人間なんだョ！
English: What!? Even though I look like this, I'm still a person, you know!

Japanese: ゴハンも食べるしコイもするし、 ヒトのモノに手だって出すサ！
English: I still eat meals like you, I fall in love... and "borrow" things from people!

Japanese: い‥‥いや、 それはちがうでしょう。
English: Um... I think your definition of "borrow" is a little off...

Japanese: オバチャン、あの女の名刺を 見たワケョ。そしたら‥‥
English: I saw that woman's business card and that's when I noticed it said,

Japanese: “いやらしい芸能カメラマン” って書いてあるじゃないか！
English: "Slimebag Celebrity Photographer Extraordinaire"!

Japanese: どんな写真を撮ってるか、 わかったモンじゃないからね！
English: Well, when I saw that, I had to know what sort of pictures she had taken!

Japanese: プロの警備員として、没収して やったんだョ！　‥‥ゴクヒで。
English: I'm a professional security guard! It's my business to know these things!

Japanese: 法廷係官！
English: Bailiff!

Japanese: すぐに‥‥すぐに、そのカメラを チェックしなさい！
English: Check this camera's photos. Hurry!

Japanese: 大至急です！
English: We must examine them at once!

Japanese: 御剣検事‥‥いかがでしたか？
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth... What do we have?

Japanese: 事件に関係ありそうな写真が ‥‥１枚だけ、あった。
English: There is only one photo that seems to be relevant to this case.

Japanese: 提出してください！
English: Please present it to the court!

Japanese: こ‥‥これは‥‥トノサマン！
English: Th-This is... This is the Nickel Samurai!

Japanese: 言ったとおりだろ！ オバチャンが見たの、ソイツだョ！
English: See, I told you! That's the guy I saw!

Japanese: ‥‥これで、証人がウソをついて いないことがハッキリしました。
English: This proves that the witness was not lying earlier about this matter.

Japanese: 証拠品《ナツミの写真》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Lotta's Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: い‥‥いかがですか、御剣検事！
English: Wh-What does all this mean, Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: 写真の人物が被告であるという 証拠はない。‥‥しかし！
English: This photo by itself does not prove that the person in it is the defendant. However!

Japanese: 王都楼氏は、調書で ハッキリと証言している。
English: In his own confession, Mr. Engarde clearly stated that...

Japanese: “事件が起こるまで、 着ぐるみは脱がなかった”と！
English: At the time of the murder, he was still in his Nickel Samurai costume!

Japanese: それならば‥‥やはり、 このトノサマンは‥‥
English: If that is the case, then... this Nickel Samurai is...

Japanese: 被告人、ということになる。
English: The defendant.

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥
English: How did it come to this...?

Japanese: これで、証拠は完全に そろったものと考えます。
English: I think this brings us to the end. We have examined every piece of evidence thoroughly.

Japanese: ‥‥いかがですか、弁護人。 最後に、あなたの意見を聞きます。
English: Final comments, Mr. Wright? The court will consider them before we close.

Japanese: この写真を、決定的な証拠と 認めますが、よろしいですかな。
English: Do you agree that this photo is decisive evidence against your client?

Japanese: （あの写真がホンモノなら‥‥ もう、逃げ道がない！）
English: (If this photo really is decisive, then we're done for!)

Japanese: （でも、ヘタに異議を申し立てれば ‥‥それはそれでイノチとりだ）
English: (But if I raise an objection here and blow it, then I would put Maya's life in jeopardy.)

Japanese: （まちがえるわけにはいかない。 コタエは‥‥たった１つ！）
English: (I can't make a mistake here! There is only one road out of this mess!)

Japanese: ‥‥ナツミさんが撮った、 この写真には‥‥
English: This photo that Lotta took... There's...

Japanese: この写真に‥‥、 不審な点はありません。
English: There is nothing strange with this photo.

Japanese: フッ‥‥
English: Hmph.

Japanese: ‥‥私が手をくだす余地も なかったようだな‥‥弁護士。
English: I have done nothing to alter this picture either, in case you're wondering, Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 弁護側も、異議はないようですね。
English: I see... And it seems the defense will rest on this matter.

Japanese: それならば‥‥ もう、審理の必要はありますまい。
English: In that case, there is no reason to further prolong this trial.

Japanese: これでよかったの‥‥？ なるほどくん！
English: Is this really alright with you, Phoenix!?

Japanese: で、でも！ この写真に、おかしい点は‥‥
English: W-Well... There's nothing wrong with this photo that I can poi--

Japanese: もう一度！ もう一度、探してみましょう！
English: Try again! Take another good look at it!

Japanese: それでもダメなら ‥‥ゴネちゃいなさい！
English: And if you still don't see anything... Do something... anything to prolong the trial!

Japanese: は‥‥はい！
English: Y-Yes, Chief!

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！ ‥‥裁判長！
English: W-Wait, Your Honor!

Japanese: なんですか、弁護人。
English: What is it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 今、判決を言うところですから、 あとにしてください。
English: I'm about to enter my verdict. Please address me with your comment afterwards.

Japanese: いやいやいや。そういうワケにも 行かないんでして‥‥。
English: Um, no, I'm sorry, but this can't wait...

Japanese: おおかた、この写真にムリヤリ モンクでもつけるつもりだろう？
English: Oh? Let me guess. You are going to point out some peculiarity with this photo?

Japanese: な、なんですって‥‥？
English: Wh-What?

Japanese: ‥‥なぜ、もっと早く 言わないのですかッ！
English: Why didn't you say so earlier!?

Japanese: ‥‥この写真には‥‥ ムジュンする点があります！
English: There's... There's something strange with this photo!

Japanese: ‥‥そう来ると思ったぞ、弁護士。
English: I knew this was coming, Wright.

Japanese: どういうことですか？
English: Your thoughts, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: この写真を認めてしまえば、 もう反論の余地はない。
English: I think we can all agree there is nothing strange with this photo.

Japanese: 弁護側としては、ムリヤリにでも、 否定せざるを得ないわけだ。
English: There is no way for the defense to debunk this photo, even with a bunker buster.

Japanese: “ムリヤリ”‥‥！ そうなのですか、弁護人！
English: "Debunk with a bunker buster"!? Is that what you're planning to do, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: と、とにかく、もう一度 写真を見てみましょう！
English: Um, anyway, please look at the photo one more time!

Japanese: あなたの立証が“ムリヤリ” でないとすれば‥‥
English: If you really believe you can honestly find something wrong with this photo,

Japanese: 反証のチャンスは、 １回でじゅうぶんでしょうな‥‥？
English: then you should only need one chance, correct...?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥
English: Um... Well...

Japanese: （まちがえるわけにはいかない！ 決定的なムジュンを示すんだ！）
English: (I have to find something wrong with this photo! I can't let this chance go by!)

Japanese: （‥‥ナツミさんの撮った 写真は、いつだってそうだ‥‥）
English: (Where in the heck did she take this picture anyway...?)

Japanese: （決定的に、ピントがズレている！ ‥‥今回だって、きっと‥‥）
English: (It's all out of focus! Why can't she take a good shot, especially when it counts!?)

Japanese: では、示していただきましょう。
English: Now then, let's hear your objection.

Japanese: この写真がはらんでいる、 決定的なムジュン点とは‥‥！
English: What about this photo is "strange"...?

Japanese: それは‥‥やはり このヒトでしょう。
English: It's this person here, of course!

Japanese: “トノサマン”‥‥ それがどうかしましたかな？
English: You mean the "Nickel Samurai"? And what is wrong with that?

Japanese: 事件があった時間、オートロさんは 控え室にいたはずです！
English: At the time of the murder, Mr. Engarde was in his own room!

Japanese: だから、このトノサマンはニセ
English: Which means this Nickel Samurai is a fa--

Japanese: そんな言いぶんが通るのなら、 キサマなどいらん！
English: If you think that's all it takes, you should reconsider your choice of occupation.
Urk.

Japanese: ‥‥というか、そもそも キサマなどいらん！
English: I think it's about time you retired!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、やはり写真には 問題はなかったようですね。
English: Hmm, it looks like there was no problem with this photo after all.

Japanese: 問題は、そこの弁護士に あったようだな‥‥。
English: The problem was with the brain of that lawyer, Your Honor.

Japanese: ま、待ってください！
English: W-Wait!

Japanese: 最後のチャンスを逃したのは あなた自身ですぞ、弁護人！
English: You are the one who let your last chance fly away from you, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: それはどうやら、このへん なのかもしれません！
English: The strange thing is somewhere around here! ...I think.

Japanese: “どうやら”‥‥？
English: "Somewhere"...?

Japanese: “このへん”‥‥？
English: "Around here"...?

Japanese: “かもしれません”‥‥？
English: "I think"...?

Japanese: ‥‥ち‥‥ ちがいましたか？
English: A-Am I seeing something you all are not...?

Japanese: ‥‥注目すべきは、 ここしかありません！
English: I would like to direct the court's attention to this one area right here!

Japanese: ‥‥な、なんですか？ そんな、足もとなど‥‥
English: Wh-What are you pointing to? His ankles...?

Japanese: 足もとを見れば、 ハッキリわかることがあります。
English: If you could see this person's ankle, that would be one thing... However you can't.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥いったい‥‥？
English: A-And...? What does that mean...?

Japanese: この写真のトノサマンは‥‥ 王都楼　真悟ではあり得ない！
English: The "Nickel Samurai" in this photo could not have been Mr. Engarde!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: ‥‥そのコンキョを 聞かせてもらおうか。
English: I wonder if you would care to elaborate... With actual facts, that is.

Japanese: トノサマンのポスターを 見ていただきましょう。
English: Let's take a look at the Nickel Samurai's poster.

Japanese: ‥‥ハカマのスソに 注目してください。
English: Please pay particular attention to the area around the bottom of the hakama.

Japanese: 足袋‥‥が見えていますね‥‥
English: His... His socks... You can see his socks...

Japanese: そのとおり！ しかし、写真のトノサマンは‥‥
English: Exactly! However, in this photo...

Japanese: アキラカに、ハカマを 引きずって歩いています！
English: The Nickel Samurai is clearly holding his hakama up just to walk!

Japanese: このムジュンが示す コタエは‥‥ひとつ！
English: There is only one explanation for this!

Japanese: このトノサマンには、被告より 背が低い人物が入っていたのです！
English: The person inside this costume is clearly much shorter than the defendant!

Japanese: （よし‥‥今度こそ、 ひっくりかえしてやったぞ！）
English: (Alright. I think I've turned things around for myself this time!)

Japanese: 気になるわね‥‥
English: That's curious...

Japanese: え？
English: Huh? What is?

Japanese: 今日の御剣検事‥‥ いやにおとなしいわ。
English: Edgeworth is unusually calm today...

Japanese: そ、そういえば‥‥
English: That's true...

Japanese: 彼は今、流れに 身をまかせているわ。
English: He's just letting the trial run itself; as if he's only along for the ride.

Japanese: 流れに身を‥‥ それって、どういうことですか？
English: "Along for the ride"...? What do you mean by that?

Japanese: ダメージを受けていない ‥‥そう考えるべきね。
English: I can only think that perhaps he doesn't feel under attack at all...

Japanese: （ダメージを‥‥受けていない！）
English: (He doesn't feel under attack!? Then, he's not taking any damage!?)

Japanese: 御剣検事！　いったい‥‥ これは、どういうことですか！
English: Mr. Edgeworth! Where does this leave us!?

Japanese: この写真の人物が 王都楼　真悟でないとしたら‥‥
English: If the person in this photo is not Matt Engarde...

Japanese: 検察側の主張は、完全に イミを失ってしまいます！
English: Then everything the prosecution has tried to prove has become meaningless!

Japanese: やはり‥‥こうなったか。
English: Hmm... I thought it would come to this.

Japanese: なんだと‥‥！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂。 ひとつ、聞きたいことがある。
English: Wright. I have something I want to ask you.

Japanese: どうやら、この写真の人物は 王都楼　真悟ではないようだ。
English: I think you have proven that the person inside this costume is not Matt Engarde.

Japanese: ‥‥とすれば。
English: In that case...

Japanese: いったい、だれなのか‥‥？
English: Who IS this a photo of...?

Japanese: （この、トノサマンの中に 入っている人物は‥‥）
English: (Who is the person wearing the Nickel Samurai costume?)

Japanese: 考えるまでもないわ。 ‥‥カンタンなコト。
English: Don't stress out over this, Phoenix. It's very simple.

Japanese: 気になるのは‥‥やはり、 あの検事さんのタイドね。
English: What you should be focused on is Edgeworth's attitude, don't you think?

Japanese: （なぜあそこまで、 落ちついていられるのか‥‥？）
English: (Yeah... Why is he so calm...?)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。 あなたの考えを聞きましょう。
English: Mr. Wright. Let's hear your thoughts.

Japanese: この写真に写っているのは、 いったい‥‥だれなのですか？
English: Who is the person in this photograph...?

Japanese: その人物が、トノサマン ‥‥なんですか？
English: Are you saying this is who you think was in the costume...?

Japanese: え、ええ‥‥たしかに、 コンキョはウスいですが。
English: Uh... Um... Well, I know there isn't a lot to back me up here, but...

Japanese: ハッ！　ウスッペラな人間性が 出たようだな、弁護士！
English: Sometimes I wonder about you. Do you just pick the first piece of evidence you see?

Japanese: まさか、こんなところで まちがえるとは思わなかったわ。
English: I can't believe it. I didn't think you would mess up here of all places...

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥ホンキで あきれてるみたいだ）
English: (Argh... And I really thought I had it!)

Japanese: では、考えなおして いただきましょうか。
English: Now then, would you care to try one more time?

Japanese: か、華宮　霧緒‥‥
English: A-Adrian Andrews...?

Japanese: ‥‥トノサマンがだれであるか？ その可能性が、最も高い人物です！
English: If you want to know who that Nickel Samurai is, it is none other than this woman!

Japanese: いったい、どんな可能性が あると言うのか‥‥？
English: And why would you say it would be Ms. Andrews!? What in the world points you to her?

Japanese: まず彼女は、背が小さい。
English: For starters, she's short.

Japanese: そして、オートロさんの控え室に 自由に出入りできた。
English: And, she can freely move in and out of Mr. Engarde's room.

Japanese: さらに‥‥彼女は、オートロさんと 食事をとっています。
English: Finally, she had dinner with Mr. Engarde that night.

Japanese: そ、それがどうかしましたか？
English: And how does that all add up?

Japanese: 彼女なら、カンタンに 手に入れることができたわけです。
English: It means that it makes it very easy for her to get a certain item.

Japanese: ‥‥オートロさんの指紋がついた ナイフを、ね。
English: ...A certain knife with Mr. Engarde's fingerprints all over it!

Japanese: 凶器の‥‥ナイフ‥‥！
English: The knife that was used as a murder weapon!

Japanese: 何が言いたい‥‥弁護人？
English: Why don't you just say what it is you want, Wright?

Japanese: （今しかない‥‥これが最後、 逆転のチャンスだ！）
English: (I have to do this now! This is my last chance to turn things around!)

Japanese: 弁護側は、 華宮　霧緒を告発します！
English: The defense motions to indict Ms. Adrian Andrews in the murder of Juan Corrida!

Japanese: 彼女は、被告人に 殺人の罪を着せようとしたのです！
English: It was Ms. Andrews who tried to frame the defendant for the crime!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!! ORDER!!

Japanese: 審理はまたしても、 予想外の展開を見せました！
English: It looks like this trial has hit a most unexpected development!

Japanese: ‥‥御剣検事！
English: Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: なんだろうか。
English: Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: 本法廷は、華宮　霧緒さんの 召喚を要求します！
English: This court is issuing a subpoena for Ms. Adrian Andrews!

Japanese: 彼女の証言を聞かないかぎり、 判決を下すことはできません！
English: A verdict cannot be passed without first hearing her testimony!

Japanese: （よし！　これで‥‥）
English: (Alright! This is it...)

Japanese: ちょっと‥‥まずい感じね。
English: This... is kind of bad for us.
Huh? What do you mean?

Japanese: これから華宮　霧緒を 証人として喚問するのなら‥‥
English: If Adrian Andrews is summoned to court as a witness...

Japanese: 手配や手つづきで、 １日はかかってしまうわ。
English: It means that the trial will go on for another day.

Japanese: ‥‥１日かかる‥‥？　あッ！
English: One more day...? Ack!!

Japanese: （今日中に判決をもぎとらないと、 真宵ちゃんが‥‥！）
English: (If I don't get a verdict TODAY, then Maya...!)

Japanese: ‥‥では、華宮　霧緒の証言は、 明日の審理で聞くことに‥‥
English: Now then, we shall set Ms. Andrews' testimony for tomorrow...

Japanese: （ど‥‥どうする！ 審理が終わっちまうぞ‥‥！）
English: (Wh-What am I supposed to do!? The judge is about to adjourn the court!)

Japanese: それでは、これで‥‥
English: Now then...

Japanese: 審理をつづけてください！ 今日中に‥‥決着を！
English: Please, Your Honor! Continue the trial! You must pass a verdict today!

Japanese: それはできません。
English: I can't do that.

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒さんが この法廷にいない以上、これ
English: We cannot hear Ms. Andrews' testimony if she is not--

Japanese: （なんとかしなければ‥‥ 審理をつづけなければ‥‥！）
English: (I have to do something... The trial must go on!)

Japanese: （‥‥しかし‥‥これ以上、 ぼくには材料がない‥‥！）
English: (But I don't have anything left... No evidence, no nothing!)

Japanese: それでは、今日の審理は ここまでとしま
English: Now then, court will adjourn for tod--

Japanese: ‥‥時間をムダにするのは シュミではない‥‥
English: I abhor wasting such valuable time...
E-Edgeworth?

Japanese: 裁判長。‥‥審理をつづけるよう、 おねがいしたい。
English: Your Honor. I request that you please continue with today's trial.

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥
English: B-But...

Japanese: こうして、法廷が予想外の 展開を見せた以上は‥‥
English: We cannot continue due to this unexpected development!

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ‥‥ “予想外の展開”‥‥？
English: Tsk, tsk. "Unexpected" development?

Japanese: ‥‥この私も、ずいぶん アマく見られたものだ‥‥
English: I think you underestimate me, Your Honor.

Japanese: ど、どういうコトですか‥‥？
English: And what do you mean by that...?

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一が苦しまぎれに 華宮　霧緒を告発する‥‥
English: That Mr. Phoenix Wright would slave his way to subpoenaing Ms. Adrian Andrews is all

Japanese: まさに、私のシナリオどおりだ。 ‥‥２０分ほど、おくれたが。
English: happening according to plan... Even if Wright was a bit slow to catch on...

Japanese: な、な、な‥‥
English: Wh-Wh-Wh...

Japanese: なんだってええェェェェッ！
English: WHAAAAAAAAAAAAT!?

Japanese: み、御剣検事！ 今の発言は、どういう‥‥
English: What is the meaning of your statement, Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: 華宮　霧緒ならば、今、 検察側の控え室にいる。
English: Ms. Adrian Andrews is currently waiting in the prosecution lobby...

Japanese: ‥‥次の証人として‥‥。
English: She is the next witness.

Japanese: （すべて‥‥ヤツの 計算どおりだったのか‥‥？）
English: (Everything... Everything was planned out in advance by that man...?)

Japanese: あの御剣検事が、華宮　霧緒を 見落とすはずがない‥‥
English: Somehow, I knew there was no way Edgeworth would overlook Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら。 戦いは終わらないようね。
English: Looks like this battle is far from over.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおり‥‥。
English: Exactly.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 次の証人として、 華宮　霧緒を入廷させましょう。
English: We will call the next witness.

Japanese: ‥‥ただし。
English: However...

Japanese: その前に、１０分間の休憩を とりたいと思います。
English: Before we proceed, we shall take a 10 minute recess.

Japanese: 御剣検事、準備のほうを よろしくおねがいします。
English: Please prepare your witness in that time, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: では、これより１０分間の 休憩にはいります！
English: The court will now take a 10 minute recess.

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: 被告人、王都楼　真悟に この場で、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Matt Engarde...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
